programme.title: Plan B
programme.desc: After a regrettable sexual encounter, a straight-laced student and her best friend have 24 hours to hunt down a Plan B pill in South Dakota.

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Country Lives
programme.desc: James and Jodie find out more about how to make their 17th-century farmhouse watertight, while Emma and James plan to take two alpacas paddling at the beach.

programme.title: Escape to Florida
programme.desc: Seán and Áine's dream becomes a nightmare. Laura juggles romance and horse dentistry during Independence Day celebrations. Film director Damian braves alligator-filled swamps.

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Alicante, the Rodriguezes search with Sara from Devon, who has decided to swap hospital beds for sun beds on the Spanish Costas after 34 years with the NHS. In Málaga, the Hamiltons search for a pooch-friendly pad with Joy and Bernd.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Mandy and Paul from Sussex would love a permanent move to the picturesque Spanish town of Dolores with their six cats and rescue dog. Jasmine Harman has found five great properties.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Debbie and Graham want to find their very own holiday home in the beautiful town of Oliva, in the south of Spain's Valencia region. Sara Damergi has conducted a rigorous search and come up with five great options for their £80,000 budget.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Craig Rowe helps recently retired Helen find her dream holiday home in La Zenia, Spain. For her budget of £105,000 Helen wants a three-bedroom house or apartment with a communal pool.

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: At Château Domaine de la Barde, a laundry room renovation has Krys and Tim in a spin. And at Château de Cézérac, Jon and Carmen encounter problems with their potager.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Married couple Mo and Anthony are looking for a holiday apartment with sea views in Torremolinos on Spain's Costa del Sol. Laura Hamilton shows them five properties for their £130k budget.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The competition comes from in and around Hastings, where the first host is 52-year-old nursery owner Louise, who's married to a farmer.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Horse-mad Emily hosts in Sussex. There's a new guest, Irish crooner Dec, who was in the Bachelors in the 1960s. Meanwhile, food snob Gary's not impressed with Emily's menu of comfort food.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Flamboyant mature student Gary plans a fun-filled evening in Hastings. There's fancy dress, diabolical dancing, fits of giggles and tactless table manners. And a guest puts his foot in it.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Hastings, former pop star Dec plans some musical surprises and a simple seaside menu, on a night featuring revelations, celebrity stories, magical musical moments and a peculiar pudding.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Beauty therapist Chiara throws an Italian dinner party in Sussex. But Irish crooner Dec compares his host to a page three glamour girl, and Gary's in a fluster when he woos Emily.

programme.title: Jamie's Ultimate BBQ
programme.desc: Jamie shows us how joyous morning grilling can be with his ultimate BBQ breakfast, before making a wood-kissed sesame chicken burger, veggie mezze and surf-and-turf mixed grill.

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: When it comes to dating etiquette, Breeze the American bulldog cross leaves a lot to be desired when he meets 31-year-old Charlotte. Will she still fall for him though?

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Miquita and Andi Oliver, Mo Gilligan, Jamie Carragher, Nick Grimshaw, Clare Balding, Shaun Ryder, Bez and loads more celebs hit the sofa for the best TV this week.

programme.title: Virgin Island
programme.desc: As the group learn the art of seduction in the penultimate phase, Jason lowers his guard, Ellen continues to flourish, and the number of ex-virgins is set to increase.

programme.title: Virgin Island
programme.desc: As the retreat concludes, Bertie heads for the bedroom with Kat for the lesson of a lifetime. Katie and Callum get together. And has Marianne finally overcome her mistrust of men?

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: How to Build a Girl
programme.desc: A teenager living with her working-class family on a council estate in Wolverhampton, England, grows up to become a popular but conflicted music journalist.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Will former mayor Tracey take the plunge with toilet roll salesman Stuart? And can burlesque dancer Double D and professional singer Lee come together and hit all the right notes?

programme.desc: A definitive one-stop shop for all things cruise, bringing you the hottest destinations and the latest deals to ensure that your next cruise ship holiday is your greatest one yet.

programme.title: Winter Road Rescue
programme.desc: Snowplough drivers and patrols are prepared to work all day and all night to keep UK roadways clear from obstruction when winter sweeps the nation. Fearless in the face of a cold front, these men and women are there to assist when disaster strikes.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Dave is on the lookout for a car driving dangerously, when suddenly it pulls out in front of him. John's 100mph blue-light run panics an unrelated driver into a crash. Veteran cop Jason's `copper's instinct' leads him to suspect a motorcyclist.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A pursuit ends in a flaming fireball for Damo and Nyci, Kev and Mark chase a gang of motorbike thieves, Jacko pursues a runaway car through a park, and Liam and police dog Vader sink their teeth into a suspected thief.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Jacko tries to stop a suspect who throws a package out of the window during the chase, Liam and sniffer dog Louis are called in to help discover why a man was in such a hurry to flee from the cops, and a young driver claims he has been coerced into breaking the law by a family member.

programme.title: Day of Anger
programme.desc: A poor street cleaner is relentlessly bullied by the people of a small western township, but things change when a ruthless gunfighter rides into the town.

programme.title: Entertainment News On 5

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: A 20-tonne digger has been written off after it fell into the Thames. Rory is dispatched to rescue a Sprinter which is blocking a bus lane, and Jim has to work fast to get a stranded HGV driver to safety.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Violent assaults against police have leapt up by a third in just 4 years - meaning that 30 officers are seriously injured in the line of duty every day.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Tim is almost mown down by a desperate driver, Adam's patience is tested to the limit by a drunken road-hog, and Ange gets spooked while on the lookout for a prowler, and a group of youths gets one over on the interceptors.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A gang of lorry thieves trigger a major manhunt, a young man is busted for heroin possession, a fraudster is taken into custody and a helicopter pursuit ends in an almighty accident.

programme.title: The Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: PC Ian Cosgrove tries to pull over a suspected drink driver refusing to stop. Meanwhile, in Warrington, PC Chris Swash is blue-lighting to a collision involving multiple vehicles. As he makes his way to the location, another collision takes place.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: We begin with an epic battle to put the brakes on a dangerous disqualified driver who leads Northamptonshire's Interceptors on a gruelling pursuit. And a drunk driver in a three-wheeled Astra mistakes Bomber and Tony for a police escort home.

programme.title: Police: Night Shift 999
programme.desc: In Gloucestershire, a police dog unit is called in to track down a pair who have gone missing after a late-night crash. Meanwhile, two drunks are restrained by officers for suspected assault.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: A psychic refuses to aid Mulder and Scully in their investigation of a serial killer preying on fortunetellers.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Worlds Toughest Prisons
programme.desc: The pre-trial prison Urso Branco is one of the worst jails in all of Brazil. Riots have been taking place here almost every year since 2002. In the past 20 years, more than 100 people have died in riots behind the walls of Urso Branco.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Trucking veteran Steve Grahame is heading to the uneven surfaces of the beach. However, it's anything but a holiday.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Super-sized transport jobs require even bigger trucks to perform them. Lifting the lid on the heavy recovery business, featuring industrial strength trucks capable of towing and lifting the biggest and heaviest machines on the nation's roads.

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.desc: A railway surveyor travels to Santiago in the hope to avenge the murder of his father and brother. Under the protection of a deputy's badge, he goes after the famous gunslinger he believes is responsible.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: A seven car motorway pile-up leaves a local council's waste truck in trouble. Fluff does his best to shift one of the heaviest loads his vehicle can lift. Meanwhile, Fluff's girlfriend Marta is called to a stranded truck in a quarry.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Craig responds to a report of an incident involving a car on fire with the driver trapped inside. In Derby, as people make their way home from a boozy night out, Alex is on the lookout for drink drivers chancing their luck.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A petrol thief runs out of gas, the helicopter follows a car thief to the fair and a drunk driver does not have to travel far to be patched up when he crashes into a surgery.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: There is a hair-raising pursuit through the market town of Ripley which sees a driver mounting pavements, hitting cars and even colliding with an ambulance. An Interceptor has a narrow escape when an out of control driver crashes into his car. A drunk driver goes for the world record in failed breathalyser tests and the helicopter is called in when a Land Rover driver decides to take a pursuit off-road.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: PC Matt Turner chases down a drink driver who's caused a collision on the morning school run, and on the M6 a broken down BMW stinks of cannabis.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: Darrell and Lisa head into Alberta and onto a trail nicknamed The Death Road. Art faces off with the ice crossing where he nearly lost his life last season, and Todd takes rookie Steph Custance under his wing as she embarks on her first journey.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: Mulder and Scully investigate the possible return of an executed prisoner who vowed to avenge his death.

programme.title: Behind Bars: Worlds Toughest Prisons
programme.desc: The Correctional Institution for Women is the oldest and largest women's prison in the Philippines, where the prisoners include petty criminals, drug dealers, murders and gang members.

programme.desc: Michelle, who runs a large bar/restaurant, hopes to have a success with her first-ever theme night - a Mexican extravaganza of food and entertainment.

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Gill prepares for her business's first-ever paying tour, taking guests to some of Malaga's treasured landmarks. But things don't get off to a great start.

programme.title: Fare Dodgers: At War With The Law
programme.desc: Investigators seek a fare dodger thought to be scamming Transport for London with over £5,000 in unpaid fares. And a man caught without a valid ticket proves uncooperative.

programme.desc: Operation Fuego's first hit involves John and South Yorkshire officers givingchase to a getaway driver in a stolen vehicle taken in a burglary, which is now on a 120mph rampagealong the M18 near Doncaster.

programme.title: Traffic Cops

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Four joyriders have a lucky escape from the wreckage of a Ford Mondeo, stolen property is found in the back of a vehicle and the interceptors have to rush to stop a suicidal man from jumping on to the M25.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Police race to intercept three men suspected of robbing a string of plumbers' merchants. A fleeing van speeds the wrong way round a major roundabout and causes a huge crash. Police investigate illegally customised motors in Basildon.

programme.title: Rogue Claimers
programme.desc: A staged crash designed to cash-in backfires badly, while a £50,000 insurance claim falls apart when on-board digital evidence raises suspicion. (S2 Ep13)

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: John offers to support Mali through his breakup and Mali reassures him that he doesn't need it - it was a mutual decision between him and Abigail.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Jo presses her father to release Tane now that there's evidence that he is innocent.

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Paramedic Oliver has a hoarse voice after being attacked by a patient, and Dr Wyne is anxious to rule out that he has been infected with HIV.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: On a 'Manic Monday' in Barnsley Casualty, a 66-year-old patient has a dangerously high heartrate, while another has the opposite - but equally dangerous - condition.

programme.title: 999: Emergency Call Out
programme.desc: For the first time in Britain, police and paramedics are joining forces to respond to 999 calls for help, using state-of-the-art fast response cars to be the first on scene at the most challenging and complex incidents.

programme.title: 999 Critical Condition
programme.desc: Barry is rushed into resus with suspected sepsis. Meanwhile, Lucy suffers significant nerve damage from a horse-riding accident that could leave her paralysed for the rest of her life.

programme.title: Ambulance: Code Red

programme.title: Skin A&E

programme.title: Babies 24/7: The Maternity Ward

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: While there's a shortage of space on specialist wards, a man is blue lit into casualty after a fall, and an ambulance rushes in Betty, 75, who has been vomiting blood.

Ben learns how to pick oysters, brew beer, trade fish and dance with the locals, finding out how it feels to truly live alone.

programme.title: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild

While staying with them, Ben learns how to chop trees and protect himself from grizzly bears.

programme.desc: Mitch (David Hasselhoff) and his crew race against time to save Hobie (Jeremy Jackson), Jackie (Susan Anton) and others trapped by an earthquake.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch and Gail's wedding plans are jeopardized when an explosion has Mitch rushing to aid the victims; Logan and Matt fight over Caroline.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch joins John Cort in a grueling dune buggy race in Mexico, unaware that John plans to smuggle artifacts across the border.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Working together for 24 hours, Mitch and Stephanie rediscover their personal relationship; Hobie has a party.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Matt befriends a young boy who is ashamed of his father's dwarfism; Caroline's infatuation with Logan causes her to miss a rescue.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch is annoyed by an arrogant FBI agent who is working undercover as a lifeguard in order to monitor a crime boss's girlfriend.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.sub-title: KGAS, the Groove-Yard of Solid Gold
programme.desc: Craziness erupts along the beach as people go after clues that could net them a $100,000 prize. With David Hasselhoff, David Charvet.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: The Santa Ana winds blow some unusual patrons onto the beach, including the boyfriend of a woman Mitch rescued years ago, and an amorous older couple.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch dives into a romance with a Hovercraft rescue expert; Matt gets an opportunity to play with the National Pro Beach Volleyball Tour.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch and the Malibu lifeguards deal with snow, romance, little people and a con artist at Christmas.

programme.title: Baywatch

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Logan is faced with two equally unpleasant choices in order to stay in the United States; Mitch's relationship with Tracy meets with his mother's disapproval.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Stephanie (Alexandra Paul), returning from her trip with Riley (Blake Gibbons), is taken hostage by an environmental extremist (Keri Jo Chapman); Mitch (David Hasselhoff) plays a practical joke on Garner (Gregory Alan-Williams) and a new lifeguard (Gretchen Palmer).

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch learns the reason for Tracy's reluctance to take their relationship further; Logan and Caroline deal with two reckless youths on wave runners.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Hobie tries to help Mitch get over Tracy's death; Caroline performs mouth-to-mouth resuscitation on a man with AIDS.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Mitch receives a mysterious package from his late father; Logan's strange reaction to a boat fire prompts Caroline to take him to a hypnotist.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: An old friend of Mitch's who is a part-time lifeguard uses overly aggressive tactics to deal with two vagrants; Logan jeopardizes his immigrant status.

programme.title: Baywatch
programme.desc: Matt and CJ help a homeless man who turns out to be former lifeguard John D. Cort; Mitch searches for a seriously ill boy's missing brother.

programme.title: Beathaichean a' Bocadaich

programme.title: Meaban and Moo
programme.desc: Tha Linda a' tadhal air Meaban is Moo gus dùbhlan a chur romhpa. An-diugh, feumaidh iad rudan a tha geal a lorg. Meaban and Moo must find white items.

programme.title: Na Clangers
programme.desc: Little Dùd needs to be where he can hoot all day. Unfortunately, he went missing from his home. However, with a little help from his new friends, he might be able to find his way back home to Planet Dùd.

programme.title: Na Clangers
programme.desc: Tha dùbhradh grèine dol bhith ann 's tha 'ad uile air bhioran mun seo! There's going to be a solar eclipse and everyone is excited about it. However, the Iron Chicken is eclipsing the eclipse.

programme.title: Clann Na Cruinne
programme.desc: Míriam feels calm and full of good vibes when she is surfing in the sea.

programme.title: Belle and Sebastian
programme.desc: While Sonaidh is busy playing with Belle, the train unexpectedly leaves without him. Sebastian and Belle must do everything they can to help Sonaidh catch the train and get back safely.

programme.title: Seonaidh
programme.desc: Timmy sees a particularly grumpy farmer giving one of the flock a hard time and decides to give him an idea of what it's like to be a sheep. However, things aren't quite so simple when the time comes to have the old farmer back.

programme.title: Uaine
programme.desc: Calum visits the Findhorn Community in Morayshire. It was started by just three people in 1962, who wanted a greener lifestyle and community and today, there are more than 400 people living there.

programme.title: Bogaisean is Gumbalan
programme.desc: Jolli is sick of her achievements being shared with Merri, so she sets out to prove herself as a solo gumbler. When she ditches her brother, he is captured by the snikes, and on her rescue mission she realises it is better when they work together.

programme.title: Miann na Maighdinn-mara
programme.desc: Kate Macleod, The Hebridean Mermaid, takes us on a journey to some of the hidden gems of the Hebrides.

programme.title: An Doimhne
programme.desc: Tha Fionn a' feuchainn ri athair a stad is tha e feumach air taic bho Na Neachdan. Fionn is trying to stop his father and needs help from the Neachdan family.

programme.title: Na Speuran
programme.desc: Tha rudan inntinneach aig an Speuradair Zach ri innse mu dheidhinn Voyager 1 agus 2. Astronaut Zach shares some interesting facts about Voyager 1 and 2.

programme.title: An Saoghal Droil aig Pòl Ploc
programme.desc: Lil Sue has started a delivery company, Deliver Sue, and in a quest to raise money for a new set of wheels, Dave lands himself with a job.

programme.title: An Saoghal Droil aig Pòl Ploc
programme.desc: Dave is thrilled when he wins an electronic home-help device called Barry. Finally, his family can boss something around other than him. However, it's not long before Barry is overwhelmed by the Spuds' demands.

programme.title: Gàrraidhean Mòra
programme.desc: Tha Màiri Rodgers a' gabhail cuairt tro Ghàrradh Threave faisg air Caisteal Dhùghlais. Mairi Rodgers explores Threave Garden near Castle Douglas. She gets some tips on caring for glasshouse plants and finds about the honey being made at the garden.

programme.title: From Ice to Fire: The Incredible Science of Temperature
programme.desc: Dr Helen Czerski explores the narrow band of temperature that has led to life on Earth. She reveals how life began in a dramatic place where hot meets cold, and how every single living creature on Earth depends on temperature for its survival.

programme.title: The Ruth Ellis Files: A Very British Crime Story
programme.desc: In April 1955 Ruth Ellis shot her lover David Blakely dead. It's a case that shocked the nation and it still fascinates today. It has its place in ushering in the defence of diminished responsibility and the abolishment of capital punishment.

programme.title: Andrew Davies Remembers... A Very Peculiar Practice
programme.desc: Writer Andrew Davies looks back on the 1980s black comedy A Very Peculiar Practice.

programme.title: A Very Peculiar Practice
programme.sub-title: A Very Long Way From Anywhere
programme.desc: Fresh-faced GP Stephen Daker starts his new job at the very peculiar Lowlands Medical Centre and discovers Jock's diagnosis of homesickness is actually a case of acute appendicitis.

programme.title: A Very Peculiar Practice
programme.sub-title: We Love You, That's Why We're Here
programme.desc: Stephen is called upon to give a lecture to the students on the first day of term but has to start dealing with the students' miscellaneous sex lives, forcing Stephen to have to ask Bob and Rose Marie for help.

programme.title: Andrew Davies: Rewriting the Classics
programme.desc: Controversial, witty, irreverent, Britain's best known screenwriter, Andrew Davies, has created some of the most iconic small-screen dramas of the past 50 years. At the age of 82 he is following his smash hit adaptation of `War and Peace' with another global epic, Victor Hugo's `Les Misérables'. As he watches the production come to life during 2018, he looks back at the influence of his childhood in Cardiff.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Rip-Off Britain
programme.sub-title: I Cancelled My Card, but Scammers Kept Spending
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Julia Somerville and Louise Minchin investigate the mysterious transactions made via mobile phone payments that happened after the customer had cancelled their bank card. Experts explain what can be done to protect customers. Also on the programme, the small business owners being blackmailed on social media with fake reviews, the fraudulent websites taking clean air zone payments, and the new technology designed to help us spot scams.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: We head to the Kent coast to revisit a renovation that's taken some time to finish. Jacqui checks out a large terrace as well as a tiny flat in a tale of two contrasting projects.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles remembers the historic day in 1972 when Llanelli defeated the mighty All Blacks at Stradey Park in one of rugby's famous upsets. He meets Roy Bergiers, the Scarlet who scored the game-winning try, and sees Roy's collection of match memorabilia that he's kept all these years. Meanwhile, the red and blue teams vie for victory at an antiques fair in Llandeilo. Expert Kate Bliss is joined by dad and daughter Rob and Charlotte, while Richard Madley captains couple Neil and Pru.

programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Briony May Williams has her work cut out on as she helps a couple who claim to have viewed over a thousand properties online in the search for their dream home in rural Suffolk. Keeping fit and healthy is a priority and so is space.

programme.desc: Will and Dom discover the noble sport of pram racing in Sussex, Kirsten performs emergency surgery on a garden gnome, and science meets art with a pair of neon sign makers.

programme.desc: A quiz show in which a team of four take on the `Bridge of Lies'. Each Bridge crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and 10 lies, and they must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies.

programme.title: Saving Private Ryan
programme.desc: Oscar-winning World War II tale combining the story of an unusual rescue mission with an overwhelmingly powerful re-creation of the D-Day landings. The powers that be want to send him home to his family.

programme.title: Rip-Off Britain
programme.sub-title: Gift Experience Voucher Left Me £200 Out of Pocket
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Julia Somerville and Louise Minchin ask whether gift vouchers for short breaks and experiences really add up. One man's weekend away would have cost him hundreds of pounds more than if he'd booked direct. And the motorist who only discovered his insurer had stopped sending documents through the post when he was pulled over by the police for driving without insurance.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin is overcome by the mould at a Southampton semi. Owain loves the 70s period charm on offer in Herefordshire. And Jacqui is in upmarket Chelsea, but the flat she's in is not as fashionable as the area... at least for now!

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Grab a cuppa and watch Roo Irvine present a special show on a subject close to her heart - Asia! From Japanese cabinets to Indian silver bowls - this show highlights some Asian gems our Bargain Hunt teams have purchased in the past and counts down the most profitable pieces at auction. Along the way, Roo also learns about the ancient art of Japanese block printing. She also looks back at when Eric Knowles met auctioneer Leon Ma to find out more about a fascinating collection of Chinese ceramics.

programme.title: Rip-Off Britain
programme.sub-title: Gift Experience Voucher Left Me £200 Out of Pocket
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Julia Somerville and Louise Minchin ask whether gift vouchers for short breaks and experiences really add up. One man's weekend away would have cost him hundreds of pounds more than if he'd booked direct. And the motorist who only discovered his insurer had stopped sending documents through the post when he was pulled over by the police for driving without insurance.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin is overcome by the mould at a Southampton semi. Owain loves the 70s period charm on offer in Herefordshire. And Jacqui is in upmarket Chelsea, but the flat she's in is not as fashionable as the area... at least for now!

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Grab a cuppa and watch Roo Irvine present a special show on a subject close to her heart - Asia! From Japanese cabinets to Indian silver bowls - this show highlights some Asian gems our Bargain Hunt teams have purchased in the past and counts down the most profitable pieces at auction. Along the way, Roo also learns about the ancient art of Japanese block printing. She also looks back at when Eric Knowles met auctioneer Leon Ma to find out more about a fascinating collection of Chinese ceramics.

programme.title: Rip-Off Britain
programme.sub-title: I Cancelled My Card, but Scammers Kept Spending
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Julia Somerville and Louise Minchin investigate the mysterious transactions made via mobile phone payments that happened after the customer had cancelled their bank card. Experts explain what can be done to protect customers. Also on the programme, the small business owners being blackmailed on social media with fake reviews, the fraudulent websites taking clean air zone payments, and the new technology designed to help us spot scams.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: We head to the Kent coast to revisit a renovation that's taken some time to finish. Jacqui checks out a large terrace as well as a tiny flat in a tale of two contrasting projects.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles remembers the historic day in 1972 when Llanelli defeated the mighty All Blacks at Stradey Park in one of rugby's famous upsets. He meets Roy Bergiers, the Scarlet who scored the game-winning try, and sees Roy's collection of match memorabilia that he's kept all these years. Meanwhile, the red and blue teams vie for victory at an antiques fair in Llandeilo. Expert Kate Bliss is joined by dad and daughter Rob and Charlotte, while Richard Madley captains couple Neil and Pru.

programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Julia Somerville and Louise Minchin ask whether gift vouchers for short breaks and experiences really add up. One man's weekend away would have cost him hundreds of pounds more than if he'd booked direct. And the motorist who only discovered his insurer had stopped sending documents through the post when he was pulled over by the police for driving without insurance.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin is overcome by the mould at a Southampton semi. Owain loves the 70s period charm on offer in Herefordshire. And Jacqui is in upmarket Chelsea, but the flat she's in is not as fashionable as the area... at least for now!

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Grab a cuppa and watch Roo Irvine present a special show on a subject close to her heart - Asia! From Japanese cabinets to Indian silver bowls - this show highlights some Asian gems our Bargain Hunt teams have purchased in the past and counts down the most profitable pieces at auction. Along the way, Roo also learns about the ancient art of Japanese block printing. She also looks back at when Eric Knowles met auctioneer Leon Ma to find out more about a fascinating collection of Chinese ceramics.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: BBC Wales Today
programme.desc: News and weather in Wales.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: Angellica Bell and Nicki Chapman join us from the RHS Chelsea Flower Show 2026 where a team of experts have top tips to help you make the most of your garden while saving you time and effort. Carol Klein celebrates the plants that will thrive without much attention, and Toby Buckland explains how to create a Chelsea-inspired, low-maintenance border. And the winner of this year's Best Balcony and Container Garden is revealed.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: The coast is a theme today as Denise Nurse helps a keen gardener move to North Yorkshire to be closer to her family and her treasured grandson.

programme.title: The Repair Shop: On the Road
programme.sub-title: Dundee - A Repair for a Scottish Football Legend
programme.desc: The team help the people trying to save Britain's oldest wooden ship, a football cap brings back treasured memories, and Lucia sees a complicated wooden puzzle at the V&A Museum.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: A quiz show in which a team of four take on the `Bridge of Lies'. Each Bridge crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and 10 lies, and they must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Susan Wokoma for the brainy quiz where the contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.desc: The latest national and international news, followed by weather.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Roman Kemp bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Follow the latest dramas of the residents of Albert Square in London's East End as they deal with life, love and loss.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: After more than 11,000km across eight countries, the teams face one final test of endurance as they race to claim victory at the finish line in Hatgal, Mongolia.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: Six months after the race, all five teams reunite to relive their 12,000km journey from Palermo to Mongolia, with unseen moments and unprecedented behind-the-scenes access.

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Features a studio audience quizzing top politicians on the events of the day. The panel surrounding presenter Fiona Bruce is composed of at least one member of the three major parties.

programme.title: Reporting Scotland
programme.desc: The latest news and weather from around Scotland.

programme.title: Scotland's Home of the Year
programme.desc: It's the grand final! Interior designer Anna Campbell Jones, architect Danny Campbell and Isle of Mull-based interior designer Banjo Beale have scoured the country in search of Scotland's home of the year. It's now down to the final six homes - the winners of each regional episode - to go head-to-head, but only one of them can take the prestigious title.

programme.title: Born to be Wild
programme.desc: Former wildlife assistant April has been newly promoted to the head of the large mammals section and, with the other team members, she anxiously awaits the influx of orphans that accompanies the arrival of March each year.

programme.title: Debate Night
programme.desc: Topical debate on the big issues affecting Scotland and beyond, with members of the public putting their questions to a panel of politicians and other public figures about the biggest stories and the things they care about the most.

programme.title: Showpeople: Licensed to Thrill
programme.desc: It's Danger Night in Bathgate, and Christian Horne is struggling to open after two days of torrential rain.

programme.title: General Assembly
programme.desc: Coverage of the General Assembly of the Church of Scotland, including films on the legacy of slavery and supporting asylum seekers.

programme.title: Scotcast
programme.desc: Conversation about Scotland's biggest news stories, hosted by Martin Geissler.

programme.title: Scot Squad
programme.desc: Officers deal with an endless Christmas, a drug dealer's infinite excuses and the limitless nonsense of local nuisance Bobby Muir.

programme.title: This is BBC Scotland
programme.desc: Launches Sunday 24 February at 7pm with comedy, drama, documentaries, news and sport.

programme.title: Reporting Scotland
programme.desc: The latest news and weather from around Scotland.

programme.title: Wild Cameramen at Work
programme.desc: Examining the challenges of filming wildlife in the sky. John Aitchison and Mark Smith reveal some of the unlikely locations they have filmed birdlife, from a Delhi rubbish dump to a motorway flyover near Birmingham.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Kirsty Wilson and Brian Cunningham show how to help the pollinators in your garden and at Beechgrove as they focus on planting that will attract bees.

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie visits a farm near Eyemouth to let the cows out for summer. Meanwhile, Rosie steps back in time in Aberdeenshire to find out what one old farm building can reveal about the past.

programme.title: Highland Cops
programme.desc: A team of police travel to the Outer Hebrides to search through the snow for a missing 16-year-old. In Shetland, the cops have to deal with Viking revellers and a drunk driver.

programme.title: Icons of Football
programme.desc: Davie Cooper holds a unique place in football folklore. This portrait of the Rangers and Motherwell legend shows the many sides to one of the greatest players of his generation.

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Steven Thompson presents highlights from the first leg of the Premiership play-off final.

programme.title: Scotcast
programme.desc: Conversation about Scotland's biggest news stories, hosted by Martin Geissler.

programme.title: Stevens & McCarthy
programme.desc: The girls in the roll van continue to tease Davie. A nightclub toilet encounter brings a surprise, and features a karaoke night with a difference.

programme.title: This is BBC Scotland
programme.desc: Launches Sunday 24 February at 7pm with comedy, drama, documentaries, news and sport.

programme.title: This Is BBC THREE
programme.desc: BBC THREE: Refreshing TV for the digital age. Tune in daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This Is BBC THREE
programme.desc: BBC THREE: Refreshing TV for the digital age. Tune in daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: The Catch-Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve heads into the heart of the Suriname jungle. He goes in search of poison dart frogs, deadly snakes, and hopes to find the elusive giant river otter.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve goes in search of the deadliest cats in Africa in the carnivore capital of Zambia. He gets up close to the rulers of the savannah: the lions and leopards.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: Alan and the final five designers head to Leeds to transform studio rental flats. They must cleverly divide open-plan spaces and inject character into show flats to entice potential renters. Former Interior Design Masters winner Lynsey Ford joins Michelle to critique the designs, and they are blown away by the dramatically different results.

programme.title: Green Book
programme.desc: Dr Don Shirley is a world-class African-American pianist, who is about to embark on a concert tour in the Deep South in 1962. In need of a driver and protection, Shirley recruits Tony Lip, a tough-talking bouncer from an Italian-American neighbourhood in the Bronx. Despite their differences, the two men soon develop an unexpected bond while confronting racism and danger in an era of segregation.

programme.title: Kidnapped by My Mum
programme.desc: At 11 years old, Alex Batty vanished on what was supposed to be a short family holiday with his mother and grandfather. An international missing persons investigation was launched, but years passed without a trace. Then, six years later, a teenager appeared on the side of a road in the French Pyrenees and told a passing driver that he'd been kidnapped. Alex, now aged 20, is ready to tell the full story about his abduction and his life on the run.

programme.title: Confessions of a Teenage Fraudster
programme.desc: A cautionary tale about one of the UK's most notorious credit card fraudsters, whose five-year crime wave began at just 16 years of age. While his contemporaries were using fake IDs to get into nightclubs, he was defrauding innocent cardholders from his desk in a call centre, laying the groundwork for a journey that would open up a world of wealth, first-class travel and an escape from the life he seemed destined for.

programme.title: Confessions of a Teenage Fraudster
programme.desc: Elliot is stealing and spending across the globe, despite a squad of investigators hot on his heels.

programme.title: Confessions of a Teenage Fraudster
programme.desc: A cautionary tale about one of the UK's most notorious credit card fraudsters.

programme.title: Stranger in My Family
programme.desc: Luke grew up in a white working-class family in Rochdale, but always felt like an outsider. He thought it might be because of his sexuality, but when he came out as gay aged 18, his parents were very supportive, and the feeling did not go away. Persistent questions about his appearance eventually inspire him to take a DNA test and the result explodes his identity, revealing a secret his mum has kept hidden since Luke's birth - his dad is not his biological father, and he is mixed race.

programme.title: This Is BBC THREE
programme.desc: BBC THREE: Refreshing TV for the digital age. Tune in daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This Is BBC THREE
programme.desc: BBC THREE: Refreshing TV for the digital age. Tune in daily from 7.00pm.

programme.title: This Is BBC TWO
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: The presenters take the prospective buyers around to different houses in the country. The desire for more room, cleaner air and less noise drives city dwellers to seek an escape to the country.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Let's hope it's not all downhill for two teams of bargain hunters as they scour the stalls of an antiques centre in Kingston upon Thames.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's railway tour of Sardinia heads inland towards the island's dramatic mountain landscapes, which he discovers were frequented by DH Lawrence.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael travels to the cultural heart of Japan, Kyoto, the city that for a thousand years was the capital. He visits a Shinto shrine, learns about a classic of Japanese literature and meets a geisha.

programme.title: Great Korean Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael's South Korean railway journey continues in Seoul. Travelling across one of the world's largest metro systems, he heads south to Suwon to visit Samsung's headquarters.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Engaging conversation about the political issues that matter to you.

programme.title: Mastermind
programme.desc: The hunt for the nation's Mastermind continues as Clive Myrie tests four more contenders. The specialist subjects are the Peter Wimsey stories of Dorothy L Sayers, Arnold Schwarzenegger, The Wire and the Falklands conflict.

programme.title: Lightning
programme.desc: Comedian Zoe Lyons presents the electrifyingly fast quiz with a £3,000 jackpot. Six plucky contestants compete across six rounds testing their general knowledge, quick thinking and speedy reactions whilst our ruthless spotlight patrols the studio.

programme.title: Clean It, Fix It
programme.desc: The Olgun family's rented two-bed flat in south west London is bursting at the seams. Their hallway is a pinch point with outdated storage and clutter in full view, so the team create a bespoke worktop and revamp the tired units to make the space fit for purpose. Then the much-used kitchen and bathroom are deep cleaned, ridding them of grease and limescale.

programme.title: Wonders of Life
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox asks how, from a lifeless cosmos, it is possible that a planet can produce the more than eight million species we know of.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel invites members of the public to bring along their antiques for examination. Interesting finds in Harrogate include naval log books from the Napoleonic era, a sumptuous silk bedcover and a beautifully crafted carriage clock.

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Members of the public are invited to try to make money out of their antiques.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Josh Jones, Molly Rainford, Mark Rhodes and Sonia take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his Sardinian railway adventure, arriving into Mamoiada to investigate the town's extraordinary pagan traditions.

programme.title: Iolo's River Valleys
programme.desc: Iolo explores the Teifi river valley, and finds a slow worm hunting, meets a coracle fisherman, is thrilled by a fishing otter, and watches a pair of kestrels at their nest.

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.sub-title: Emotional Recognition...Ok Computer, How do I Feel?
programme.desc: Mathematician and Author Hannah Fry explores the powerful new technologies set to shape society, and whether that future is one that we want. Our future doesn't just happen, we make it.

programme.title: The Secret Genius of Modern Life
programme.desc: Hanna uncovers the wild origins of the modern-day rollercoaster and gets the inside story on the UK's newest, tallest and fastest coaster - Thorpe Park's Hyperia.

programme.title: RHS Chelsea Flower Show
programme.desc: The annual Royal Horticultural Society Chelsea Flower Show is considered the most famous flower show in the United Kingdom, attracting visitors from all over the world.

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: A surgeon must remove part of his patient's tongue and reconstruct it to save her from an aggressive cancer, and surgeons perform a rare simultaneous pancreas and kidney transplant.

programme.title: Peelers: The PSNI for Real
programme.desc: Anna and Perri race to a violent offender, Tom consoles a woman whose loved one has been hit by a taxi. The Peelers control an angry crowd outside a sex offender's home.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Featuring extensive coverage of the day's top national and international news stories, along with explanations about the context and potential aftereffects.

programme.title: BBC Weather
programme.desc: The latest weather forecast.

programme.title: Unspun World with John Simpson
programme.desc: Unspun World provides an unvarnished version of the week's major global news stories - reliable, honest and essential viewing with the BBC's world affairs editor, John Simpson.

programme.title: The Turning
programme.desc: Kate Mandell takes a job as a nanny for two young orphans at an isolated Gothic mansion in the Maine countryside. She soon learns that the children - Miles and Flora - are emotionally distant and unstable. When strange events start to plague Kate and the siblings, she begins to suspect that the estate's dark corridors are home to a malevolent entity.

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Current affairs programme, featuring interviews and investigative reports on all aspects of life in Britain.

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: It's the final week of heats, which leaves just 12 amateur cooks still with a chance of storming their way to MasterChef stardom. It's no easy journey as the first group of six are about to find out. They must win over the judges and earn the right to wear a MasterChef apron. With the competition kicking up a gear, the contestants are battling for a place in the last quarter-final.

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: Hannah embarks on a journey to investigate her own biological age and whether it can really be controlled. On the island of Okinawa in Japan, she sees the key to some of the healthiest old women in the world.

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay, with Paul Gorton in tow, challenges Anneka Rice, comedian Shane Todd and DJ Remi Burgz to separate the real from the ridiculous. Today's destination is Jedburgh.

programme.title: Bing

Bing's frustrations grow when Pando jumps on first to 'bloop' his ticket, pushes Bing's hand to 'bloop' his too and sits in the window seat first. To make matters worse, the bus breaks down, making Bing very disappointed! But the busdriver lets Bing sit in the driver's seat, beep the horn and use her special driver's ticket activation card!

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are having a picnic and notice that the chickens eat differently than they do. Duggee has his eating badge and can explain all the different ways animals eat. Some animals eat slowly like cows, who eat all day long. But others eat quickly like frogs. Some, like goats, are always eating, while tortoises can go a whole year without food. Some animals eat food that is very high up, like giraffes, and some eat food that is very low down, like moles.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Dad agrees to take Bluey and Bingo to the shops to buy an ice cream if they can get him into the car. The only problem is, he's just entered `Ragdoll' mode.

programme.desc: It's pandemonium at the Alphablocks' animal sing-song. S has a special surprise in store. This episode has fun with the words cat, dog, cod, tag, jog, jig, mop, moo, moon, spoon and zoom.

programme.title: Nick Cope's Popcast
programme.desc: Nick and Norman take a road trip to visit Norman's family over at Popcast Bay and meet some other characters on the way.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.sub-title: Top of the Pups: Old MacDonald
programme.desc: Lord Fingal sings about all the animals as he takes the Squirrels on a tour around Old MacDonald's farm.

programme.title: Playtime Towers
programme.desc: Bella plays with her favourite cup - it makes the perfect drum for a sing along with Lift. Together, they visit Kingsley and Queenie who play the 'Tea Party Dash', but who will win?

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Sula and Bing tag their favourite things around the house with heart-shaped stickies. Sometimes they have different favourites and sometimes their favourite things are the same. But when Sula tags Hoppity Voosh, Bing is upset because Hoppity is his most favourite of favourites and he's not sure he wants to share him.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say 'pumpkin', and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. There is an orange pumpkin, a bumpy pumpkin and a pumpkin with lots of seeds. The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dee's friend finally succeeds in saying today's word.

programme.title: Lu & the Bally Bunch
programme.desc: When Declan struggles on his first visit to the pool, Lu puts her over-enthusiasm to one side and comforts him.

programme.title: Kiri and Lou
programme.desc: Kiri, Lou and Sorry have a running race, and Kiri is shocked to discover that their new friend is way faster than she is.

programme.title: Froglets
programme.desc: The Froglets transform kitchen cloths into mats for a day of fun yoga and gymnastics.

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos learn about popping when they encounter a machine that makes bubbles. Back in the Boat, Peekaboo interrupts teatime when he blows some bubbles of his own.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.sub-title: We're All Friends: Where We Live
programme.desc: Maddie and friends explore a town, while Mr Tumble has an important parcel to deliver.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky, Dipsy, Laa-Laa and Po take it in turns to listen to music through a pair of headphones. The Teletubbies love dancing to the music they hear.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear welcome babies Evie, Gracie, Luke, Hunter, Thea, Benjamin and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Inside everyone's bags are different types of toy transport which are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding safe, baby appropriate toy vehicles inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby!

programme.title: Tiny Tunes
programme.desc: Lisa and the children make natural crowns and sing and sign 'Sing a Song of Sixpence'.

programme.title: I Can Grow It
programme.desc: Adrian, Mia and Aaron go on a 'tree-mendous' garden adventure to discover the magical powers of trees.

programme.title: Monster Makes
programme.desc: Rebecca has a new pet plant called Stan. Murphi is very pleased to meet Stan - he loves plants and jungles. This gives him a monster idea for the first make - a terrifically tropical bug made from a reused plastic bottle. The Mini Monsters make bottle fish, and Rebecca's great aunt makes a silly plant for her pet bug. The Recycling Express brings some egg boxes to the attic - just the thing Murphi needs to make a snippy snappy egg box crocodile. The Tiny Helpers add the teeth and eyes.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger the Tricarrotops explores the world of the crunchiest era, going on adventures throughout the colourful Vegesaur Valley.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: As Honey waits impatiently for her appointment with Dr Bingo, the family distracts her by pretending to work in the hospital.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: As Peggee and the Hummingbirds journey up the river to deliver a parcel, they have fun spotting and avoiding the wildlife that they meet along the way. All while Otti tries to avoid playing with the boat whistle!

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Step to the left and jump to the beat, you better clap your hands and shuffle your feet, it's Duggee on the dance floor.

programme.title: Our Family
programme.sub-title: Iona and Elba's Ice Cream Parlour
programme.desc: Iona and Elba create a pretend ice cream shop and make Mum a heart-shaped ice cream cake. Then it's time for some real ice cream with sprinkles for decoration. On a family walk in the woods, Iona and Elba discover a trail of fairy clues to follow.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Iaia is cooking for us today and he is making a delicious stew from Guinea-Bissau called Kansiye. The dish is made with lentils, although not all of his friends are sure about the taste and smell of them when they try them before the meal. Let's hope Iaia's cooked recipe, served with rice and carrots, changes their minds. Before serving, Iaia puts a vase of olive branches on his table, as these are a symbol of Guinea-Bissau.

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie finds out about cars. She heads to a car engine factory and with her special cameras finds out how blocks of aluminium metal are melted and made into a car engine. Along the way she meets some robots that help deliver the car parts around the factory! Next, Maddie finds out how cars are loaded onto a car transporter.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Dan and Jamie ride the chair lift to the top of the mountain to warn some hikers that the resort is closing because of a storm. They may or may not get everyone off the mountain safely in time.

programme.title: Go Wild
programme.sub-title: Aneeshwar and Lizzie: Macaw and Agouti
programme.desc: Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet a flock of boisterous, bright green macaws. They get to see these colourful and chatty characters use their especially hard tongues to feast on nuts. Next, Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet two agoutis, or try to.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Mrs Hobbs supervises Waffle at the Brooklyn-Bell house. Waffle wants to join Evie at school and tries to escape. Later, the family look at photos and Evie asks about her birth Dad.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: After a stormy night on Puffin Rock, the puffin burrow is completely soaked. Papa suggests a family day out, while the burrow dries.

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: In this upbeat, hip hop song, the Small Potatoes introduce themselves and sing about their unique personalities.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: Stripes worries that the Sundial Stage isn't loud enough when his guitar hero comes to play a gig. With Yukee's help, Stripes is determined to turn the volume up to 11, but is that too loud?

programme.title: Maddie and Triggs
programme.desc: Maddie accidentally breaks a piece of pottery that Mum made, but instead of doing the right thing and owning up, she starts to worry about how bad everything will now go!

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Happy has always loved the water, even though it makes a mess. But while swimming around in his paddling pool at the playhouse, Duggee reminds the Squirrels that there was a time when Happy feared water. On Happy's first day, his mum drops him off and he is excited. He goes to play with Norrie and Roly and has loads of fun baking, playing with toys and jumping over puddles, but Happy can't do it.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: It's a lovely day for a picnic, but one of the flapjacks is missing. Three may have forgotten what Numberblocks do best.

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: From A to Z, every Alphablock has fun discovering the magic of wordplay. When T gets stuck on top of a mountain, A, E, I, O and U come to the rescue.

programme.title: Something Special: We're All Friends
programme.desc: Mr. Tumble helps Grandad Tumble water his vegetables - and lots of splashing fun is had! Meanwhile, Justin and his friends have fun gardening and making a scarecrow.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies go on a day trip to the Hidey Hup. They take turns on the Tubby Bouncy, the Tubby Slidey and the Tubby Spinny, before they go on the Tubby Uppy Downy together.

programme.title: Ranger Hamza's Eco Quest
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers discover how reeds and rushes play an important role in the environment. They find out that they help create wetland habitats because they can grow in water and on land.

programme.title: Football Fantastics
programme.desc: Theo turns an errand to fetch a new ball into an imaginative quest to find a lion at the Den. Meanwhile, Barry's practising being a lion mascot for the day. Will the two meet?

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Motorbike rider Grace shows off three amazing machines that all belong in the swamp. First Grace goes for a ride in an airboat and shows off an unusual corkscrew-propelled machine, and then goes in a swamp buggy.

programme.title: Dodge's Pup School
programme.desc: Dodge is given the job of tidy monitor but accidentally knocks over the class art project.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster has to overcome his fears of the big slide to rescue Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny when she gets scared. You see sometimes, when the big slide seems scary, it's easier to face it with a friend.

programme.title: Octonauts: Above & Beyond
programme.desc: Professor Natquik finds a red fox has moved into his home as a storm traps them both in!

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Unexpected Hero
programme.desc: When Peter invites Jeremy Fisher along for a garden raid, the melodramatic frog gives him more than he bargained for.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are crafting in the Clubhouse when Betty saves Tag from throwing water across the table instead of glitter. Tag, although full of enthusiasm and energy, has not always been the best listener. He was great at discovering new things, and everyone wanted to join in with him - but getting easily distracted would land him in an array of trouble.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Vida helps Birdie manage his asthma and perform the coolest dance ever!

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Supertato runs in fright when Pumpkin tries to make friends with him, but he later realises she needs his help as she's stuck in the air hanging from balloons set up by Evil Pea.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: When a lost bird takes a liking to Bo, it is up to Mojo to fly it to its home. But it's a stormy trip, and Bo will need to do some flying of his own!

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Dad agrees to take Bluey and Bingo to the shops to buy an ice cream if they can get him into the car. The only problem is, he's just entered `Ragdoll' mode.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey and Bingo spend the day at Nana's and want everyone to play charades, but their cousins Muffin and Socks are too young to understand the rules. Nana must quickly think of how to keep the game on track.

programme.title: Zog
programme.desc: Pearl accidentally gives Fizz and Zog a laughing disease called the draggles. The doctors must create an emotion potion before every dragon chuckles themselves to exhaustion.

programme.title: Balamory
programme.desc: With the harbour master away, Penny Pocket tends to his beloved pet plant, Colin - she must get him better!

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Connie and Ruby move into Maple Close with their dog, Jelly. Mrs Hobbs can't help but express her disapproval. She hates dogs and now there'll be another one living in close proximity. Waffle is not allowed to go on a family camping trip but accidentally goes on a different adventure!

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: Rafi, Ruby and Finn struggle to score in basketball, so Rafi wishes for a magic ball. At first, it's fun, as every shot goes in - even trick throws. But the constant winning becomes boring, and the ball bounces endlessly into the hoop by itself. The trio try to contain it, but it escapes again and again. Finally, they return to playing with the ordinary ball, and Finn sinks a real shot, proving true victory is earned.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.sub-title: It's Time to See a Rainbow
programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran get caught in the rain at the park. They shelter under the bandstand and JoJo spots a rainbow. JoJo discovers that rainbows are only visible when it's rainy and sunny at the same time.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Sarah and Duck are trying to exercise but the neighbours need their help.

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Tiny has invited the Wols for a day out on the Clangers Planet. She has planned a whole lot of activities for them all. She is going to show them her bedcave, introduce them to everyone they haven't met before, give them soup to eat and then end with a wonderful musical extravaganza. But she hadn't counted on the Wols curiosity and soon the Wols are leading Small and Tiny a merry chase. But all's well that ends well - at least until Major Clanger decides to join in the music.

programme.title: Beddybyes
programme.desc: Join MeMo and BaBa as they encounter all kinds of fantastical beings that encourage good nutrition, mindfulness, and creativity, all the while winding down towards sleep.

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Pontipines are taking their dinner for a walk in the garden. Here comes the Ninky Nonk. The Pontipines take their dinner aboard the Ninky Nonk. What a lovely dinner.

programme.title: Cbeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.sub-title: K-O:Zayn Malik - Music is in Everything
programme.desc: Zayn Malik celebrates the joy of music in this bedtime story.

Gordon has one last chance to bring them all around and make the restaurant a success.

programme.title: Cheers

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray is ready to speak with Ally about the facts of life only to find that Ally's interest has changed from where babies come from to the meaning of life.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra decides to run for school president but Ray isn't sure he can handle the twins by himself during all those meetings so he votes against her.

programme.desc: When Ray finally feels comfortable enough to call Debra's mother `mom', he wants Debra to reciprocate and start calling Marie `mom', but, when Debra finally gets the words out, she is completely stunned at Marie's reaction.

programme.title: Frasier

programme.desc: Pleasantly surprised by the woman Roz set him up with on a blind date, Frasier ends up competing with Bulldog for her attentions.

programme.desc: Frasier seeks help deciphering a recurring dream from friends and family.

programme.desc: Channel 4's midday news summary.

programme.title: Help! We Bought a Village

programme.title: Escape to Florida
programme.desc: In Miami, Charmaine is running a multimillion-pound business from her bedroom while homeschooling her daughter. Outside Orlando, Gary and Maria are expecting more grandchildren.

programme.title: Chateau DIY

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: In Almería, Sharon and Shaun Garner are channelling a higher power, hoping to lead reiki master Cathy to her ideal property. With a top budget of £140,000, the Garners hunt with Cathy for a second home for visiting friends and family in the sun.

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Includes sport and weather.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Having moved from Cape Town to Cardiff, Tish and Ramone's new home is within touching distance... and so is a ceiling. In Newport, will Gemma and Ellis fall for the same house?

programme.title: Falling
programme.desc: Anna attempts to adjust to the new path she has chosen but comes face-to-face with those from her past who disapprove. As gossip spreads throughout the community, David tries to ignore the noise. But demons from his past threaten to rear their heads.

programme.title: Marilyn and the Mob
programme.desc: Marilyn Monroe was the brightest star of fifties Hollywood. Beneath the glitz was an industry shaped by the mob. Did Marilyn pay dearly in a brutal game of power and betrayal?

programme.title: Marilyn and the Mob
programme.desc: Marilyn Monroe travels to Nevada with Frank Sinatra, placing her amid mobsters and dangerous rumours. Days later, she's found dead. Was her death just an accident?

programme.title: In the Footsteps of Killers
programme.desc: Notorious cold cases are reopened. In 1993, a Lambeth Council employee was beaten to death in his Clapham home. Was it personal or were dark forces at his workplace involved?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon visits Bazzini restaurant in Ridgewood, New Jersey. Now, he finds everyday tasks overwhelming and his passion for food is in danger of being extinguished by the daily grind. However, passion isn't the only problem in this failing restaurant.

programme.title: The Last Tree

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Country Lives

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Things are not exactly as they seem when Norm announces that he's been hired by a prestigious Boston accounting firm.

programme.title: Cheers

programme.title: Cheers
programme.sub-title: A House Is Not a Home
programme.desc: Sam and Diane buy their dream house, but the move is jeopardised by Diane's sympathy for the plight of the previous owners.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray is ready to speak with Ally about the facts of life only to find that Ally's interest has changed from where babies come from to the meaning of life.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Debra decides to run for school president but Ray isn't sure he can handle the twins by himself during all those meetings so he votes against her.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: When Ray finally feels comfortable enough to call Debra's mother `mom', he wants Debra to reciprocate and start calling Marie `mom', but, when Debra finally gets the words out, she is completely stunned at Marie's reaction.

programme.desc: Frasier seeks help deciphering a recurring dream from friends and family.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel's new boss and colleague both smoke. programme.title: Friends

programme.title: Criminally Funny

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: It's Barney's birthday, and after a pit-stop at the iconic Niagara Falls, the Walshes head to Lake Placid to recreate their very own `Miracle on Ice'. As a treat that night, Barney swaps the RV bunks for beds at the Trapp Family Lodge. The next day, armed with crampons and ice picks, Brad and Barney take on an frozen rock face before jumping into sleds with some four-legged friends and riding through a winter wonderland in Vermont.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: On the final leg of their US road trip, Brad and Barney take rally cars for a spin on the ice, before settling the score at Moose Alley bowling in Maine. To help them with the extreme weather, Barney's arranged for some wilderness training with expert Joel Graves, before an early start as the boys go lobster fishing off Swan's Island. Finally, to round off an epic adventure, the duo settle down to a good old sing-song.

programme.sub-title: The One Where the Monkey Gets Away

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Evil Orthodontist
programme.desc: Rachel gets back together with her ex-fiancé Barry, even though he's engaged to her best friend Mindy. Meanwhile, Chandler agonises over a wonderful woman who fails to call him after their first date.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Fake Monica
programme.desc: Someone is pretending to be Monica, using her credit card numbers and having the sort of fun that Monica wishes she could have.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Ick Factor
programme.desc: Monica discovers that she's developed a highly mature approach to her latest love affair, and Ross finds out that he's being mistaken for a male prostitute.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: When Carol gives birth, Ross and Susan almost miss the event when Phoebe accidentally locks them in a cupboard together to put a stop to their constant bickering. Meanwhile, Joey befriends an unmarried pregnant woman and ends up helping her to give birth.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Rachel Finds Out
programme.desc: Ross has been in love with Rachel since ninth grade but has always kept it a secret. Now the truth accidentally comes out, but as Rachel rushes to the airport to greet Ross on his return from a business trip, she discovers that work is not all he's been getting up to while he's been away.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Ross's New Girlfriend
programme.desc: Ross comes back from China with his new girlfriend Julie, and Rachel has a really hard time dealing with it. She winds up sleeping with Paolo and hates herself for it. Joey persuades her to tell Ross how she feels, but when Ross tells her he is in love with Julie, she can't bring herself to do it.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Breast Milk
programme.desc: While Carol and Susan are away, Ross and the friends babysit his newborn son Ben, but Ross is disgusted when Phoebe tests the temperature of some bottled breast milk on her wrist. Meanwhile, Joey competes with a fellow cologne salesman.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: When downstairs neighbour Mr Heckles dies, the friends clean out his apartment, but Chandler becomes convinced his hyper-critical nature will mean he ends up alone and sad, just like Heckles.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Everyone is surprised when Phoebe's husband - a gay Canadian ice dancer - shows up, as nobody knew she was married. Elsewhere, Chandler reveals his third nipple, and Ross' new relationship is under attack, unbeknown to him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Five Steaks and an Eggplant
programme.desc: Monica gets promoted and invites the friends out for dinner to celebrate, but problems arise when the difference in their salaries becomes apparent and the group splits in two. Meanwhile, when Chandler sets a woman up to get a date with her, a case of mistaken identities means that he discovers what she really thought of him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Baby on the Bus
programme.desc: Joey and Chandler babysit Ben, who turns out to be a babe-magnet until they accidentally leave him on a bus. Meanwhile, Phoebe loses her job as the singer at Central Perk to Stephanie.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Finds Out
programme.desc: Ross and Julie announce they are getting a cat together, while Rachel gets drunk on a date and all she talks about is Ross. She decides to get closure by calling him and telling his machine she is over him. Meanwhile, Chandler regrets asking Monica to help him lose weight.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross agonises over whether he should stay with Julie or date Rachel. The gang encourage him to compare the two, so he starts with a list of Rachel's negative qualities. Unfortunately, Rachel sees the list.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney Walsh are back on the road for another epic road trip, and this time they're in for a real culture shock as they head further afield to Latin America. The boys are in Mexico, where they take on Lucha Libre wresting.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The Walshes continue their Latin American road trip. They're still in Mexico and on the side of a mountain - we rejoin them to see if Bradley can complete the final part of a challenge which requires him to swing across the top of the mountain with nothing but a terrifying drop below him.

programme.desc: A topical panel show based on a huge series of opinion polls carried out around Britain. Guests David Hasselhoff, Rachel Riley, Rob Beckett and Justin Moorhouse join the regulars.
