programme.desc: Rachel gets back together with her ex-fiancé Barry, even though he's engaged to her best friend Mindy. Meanwhile, Chandler agonises over a wonderful woman who fails to call him after their first date.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Fake Monica
programme.desc: Someone is pretending to be Monica, using her credit card numbers and having the sort of fun that Monica wishes she could have.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Ick Factor
programme.desc: Monica discovers that she's developed a highly mature approach to her latest love affair, and Ross finds out that he's being mistaken for a male prostitute.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: When Carol gives birth, Ross and Susan almost miss the event when Phoebe accidentally locks them in a cupboard together to put a stop to their constant bickering. Meanwhile, Joey befriends an unmarried pregnant woman and ends up helping her to give birth.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Rachel Finds Out
programme.desc: Ross has been in love with Rachel since ninth grade but has always kept it a secret. Now the truth accidentally comes out, but as Rachel rushes to the airport to greet Ross on his return from a business trip, she discovers that work is not all he's been getting up to while he's been away.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Ross's New Girlfriend
programme.desc: Ross comes back from China with his new girlfriend Julie, and Rachel has a really hard time dealing with it. She winds up sleeping with Paolo and hates herself for it. Joey persuades her to tell Ross how she feels, but when Ross tells her he is in love with Julie, she can't bring herself to do it.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Breast Milk
programme.desc: While Carol and Susan are away, Ross and the friends babysit his newborn son Ben, but Ross is disgusted when Phoebe tests the temperature of some bottled breast milk on her wrist. Meanwhile, Joey competes with a fellow cologne salesman.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: When downstairs neighbour Mr Heckles dies, the friends clean out his apartment, but Chandler becomes convinced his hyper-critical nature will mean he ends up alone and sad, just like Heckles.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Everyone is surprised when Phoebe's husband - a gay Canadian ice dancer - shows up, as nobody knew she was married. Elsewhere, Chandler reveals his third nipple, and Ross' new relationship is under attack, unbeknown to him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Five Steaks and an Eggplant
programme.desc: Monica gets promoted and invites the friends out for dinner to celebrate, but problems arise when the difference in their salaries becomes apparent and the group splits in two. Meanwhile, when Chandler sets a woman up to get a date with her, a case of mistaken identities means that he discovers what she really thought of him.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Baby on the Bus
programme.desc: Joey and Chandler babysit Ben, who turns out to be a babe-magnet until they accidentally leave him on a bus. Meanwhile, Phoebe loses her job as the singer at Central Perk to Stephanie.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One Where Ross Finds Out
programme.desc: Ross and Julie announce they are getting a cat together, while Rachel gets drunk on a date and all she talks about is Ross. She decides to get closure by calling him and telling his machine she is over him. Meanwhile, Chandler regrets asking Monica to help him lose weight.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross agonises over whether he should stay with Julie or date Rachel. The gang encourage him to compare the two, so he starts with a list of Rachel's negative qualities. Unfortunately, Rachel sees the list.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: Bradley and Barney Walsh are back on the road for another epic road trip, and this time they're in for a real culture shock as they head further afield to Latin America. The boys are in Mexico, where they take on Lucha Libre wresting.

programme.title: Bradley and Barney Walsh: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The Walshes continue their Latin American road trip. They're still in Mexico and on the side of a mountain - we rejoin them to see if Bradley can complete the final part of a challenge which requires him to swing across the top of the mountain with nothing but a terrifying drop below him.

programme.title: Michael McIntyre's Big Show
programme.desc: `Doctor Who' star Bradley Walsh bravely hands over his phone to play Send To All, pop sensation Kylie Minogue helps Michael surprise members of the audience before performing a beautiful ballad with Jack Savoretti, and a pilot receives an aeroplane-themed surprise to become the latest Unexpected Star of the Show.

programme.title: Teachers
programme.desc: Ms Snap wonders about the possibility of motherhood and freezing her eggs. Ms Watson pushes her class to win the science fair.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: A topical panel show based on a huge series of opinion polls carried out around Britain. These `Cat Polls' are primed to root out the important issues of the day, like what is Britain's most unwanted wedding gift or who the British public would most like to have round for dinner. Guests David Hasselhoff, Rachel Riley, Rob Beckett and Justin Moorhouse join the regulars.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats
programme.desc: Host Jimmy Carr delves into the world of opinion polls with team captains Sean Lock and Jon Richardson who are joined by guests Johnny Vegas, Vernon Kay, Paul Chowdhry and Katherine Ryan.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: Host Aisha Tyler joins comic regulars along with special guests comic Gary Anthony Williams and illusionists Penn and Teller as they perform improvised sketches prompted by ideas from the audience.

programme.title: Whose Line Is It Anyway?
programme.desc: Highlights of this episode, include guest stars include Cedric the Entertainer and comic Gary Anthony Williams.

programme.title: South Park
programme.desc: The off-colour adventures of a group of foul-mouthed elementary school boys in South Park, Colorado. The students, including, Eric Cartman, Stan Marsh, Kyle Broflovski and Kenny McCormick target every conceivable group in outrageous ways.

programme.desc: Law enforcement officers pursue fugitives on the run. A getaway driver smashes into three police cruisers and a bystander's SUV while leading officers on a high-speed, car park chase.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers: Dream Car

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers

programme.desc: The team races to get a BMW wrapped in bright pink for a celebrity client. And Dean gets a surprise when he visits a collection of classic Porsches.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters: Classic Cars
programme.sub-title: Ford Fiesta Supersport And Lotus Elise
programme.desc: The guys are in Dundee to view a Lotus Elise S1. Drew is furious at numerous modifications made to the car, including stickers and a wing.

programme.title: Shed & Buried
programme.desc: In the first episode, they travel from Cornwall to Oxfordshire to rummage around in some of Britain's most amazing sheds in search of a nice little earner. Their discoveries include vintage tractors, engines and a clutch of historic motorbikes.

programme.title: Shed & Buried
programme.desc: Henry and Sam are rummaging again...this time they meet Phil in sunny Buckinghamshire, where they find stationary engines, traction engines and vintage petrol cans - but is there anything for the boys to restore and flog?

programme.title: Richard Hammond's Workshop
programme.desc: With plans for a live restoration at CarFest to restore the cog's mojo, as well as a new girlfriend for `Oliver', Richard has concerns for the workshop's prospects. Even with a higher rate job in hand, the future doesn't look certain.

programme.title: Roadworthy Rescues
programme.desc: Derek enlists Steve Dulcich to help put the muscle back into a derelict Dodge Coronet. But with a completely seized drivetrain, the guys will have to replace everything on this car if they hope to make the 500-mile road trip back to Nashville.

programme.title: Shed & Buried: Classic Cars
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Fuzz Townsend are back on the road, but this time they're looking for iconic vintage vehicles to restore and sell for a tidy profit.

programme.title: Ant Anstead Master Mechanic
programme.desc: Ant hopes to sweet-talk a few bigwigs into giving him permission to take his completed Alfa 158 to the US Grand Prix in Austin. However, the car isn't done, and his arm still isn't fully healed, leaving the heavy lifting to his crew.

programme.title: Ant Anstead Master Mechanic
programme.desc: Ant's ambitious Alfa build is in the home stretch, but his new baby could come any day now. Ant gets good news about his plan to unveil the car at the US Grand Prix, but a million critical details must still be sorted out.

programme.title: Massive Engineering Mistakes
programme.desc: A deadly design flaw results in a horrifying rush hour bridge collapse that tragically leaves 13 dead in Minneapolis; using the latest science, experts investigate to reveal what went so catastrophically wrong on one of Minnesota's busiest crossings.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters: Classic Cars
programme.desc: Paul and Drew set their sights on a Lancia Montecarlo. This classic car needs serious work, but the guys are still willing to offer £7,000 for it.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides: Road to the Ridler
programme.desc: The team passes a huge milestone, as the body and chassis are merged with mock-ups of the other major elements of the car.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike and Elvis are determined to celebrate a dying breed, which is the iconic V8 engine. They've chosen a classic sports car in order to do it, being the Mercedes SL55.

programme.title: Shed & Buried
programme.desc: Today, Henry and his ace mechanic mate Sam Lovegrove are in Surrey where they find Pontiac ambulances, mysterious automotive sculptures, and strangely well preserved motorbikes. Later, in Cornwall, they meet a collector whose cottage is like a transport museum, chock full with memorabilia, penny farthings and lights of all description.

programme.title: Shed & Buried
programme.desc: Bike guru Henry Cole and ace mechanic Sam Lovegrove are off to Cornwall. They meet a collector whose cottage is like a transport museum, chock full with memorabilia, penny farthings and lights of all description.

programme.title: Bitchin' Rides: Road to the Ridler
programme.desc: Dave agonises over the colour choice for the Ridler build. The previous five winners were all yellow, but Dave decides to go in another direction.

programme.title: Street Outlaws: Locals Only
programme.desc: In Minnesota, Farmtruck and AZN pick eight local small-tire racers including a Canadian father-son team to compete for $5K. Then negotiations with No Prep Queen Lizzy Musi prove that when small tires take on big tires, every second counts.

programme.title: Massive Engineering Mistakes
programme.desc: Experts look at engineering blunders that have either caused catastrophes or are disasters waiting to happen. They use cutting-edge technology to examine what went wrong and to figure out if they can be fixed.

programme.title: Shed & Buried
programme.desc: Series in which Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove nose around people's sheds and garages in search or automotive bargains. programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly discovers the complicated world of adult relationships is filled with minefields when she opens her mind to dating; Barry discovers a new passion.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay continues his travels across the United States, travelling to New Jersey to try to snatch Italian restaurant Leone's from the jaws of failure. Owner Leone had a thriving business, but after a sudden illness she spent two years in hospital in a coma. When she returned, she was heartbroken to discover that her lazy son Michael had run all her efforts and the restaurant into the ground.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Harry wants a royal wedding and to be King for the day and plans to make his no-nonsense bride his Queen. But, bride-to-be Charlotte wants something low key.

programme.desc: The Hutchinson family are faced with a devastating reality. Meanwhile, Charlie prepares to face the music, and Beth extends an olive branch to her estranged brother.

programme.title: Hollyoaks

programme.desc: A short, cheeky clip from Rude Tube's naughty little sister Rude-ish Tube, featuring sheep.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Orange County, Southern California, to try to save an Italian restaurant from imminent destruction. Luigi's was a successful restaurant with a great reputation until Dominic decided to retire to Italy and brought in son Tony to help run it. Familial fighting and an unappetising menu of 126 items have let the restaurant down the drain. Can Gordon fix it, or is Luigi's beyond repair?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In Atlanta, Gordon helps first-time restaurant owners Richard and Jorge. Their restaurant, Park's Edge, has been open for three years but is in big trouble due to constant fighting between them, poor food and a bad vibe in the tight-knit neighbourhood. Gordon comes up with a brilliant plan to resolve the issue within the community, but then a rogue chef threatens to ruin everything.

programme.desc: Homer is arrested for an insurance scam that Moe hatched to impress his new girlfriend.

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.title: The Simpsons

programme.desc: Jake is frustrated when a detective from Major Crimes takes over a murder case that he is close to solving and steals his thunder. Determined to outwit `The Vulture', he enlists his colleagues to help him find the murder weapon and close the case.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake bets Captain Holt that he can steal his Medal of Valor before midnight and Amy must overcome her hatred of Halloween.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Jay misinterprets Gloria's anger over a dream she thinks he had for another offence he committed.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Luke competes against the son of Phil's nemesis in a wrestling match.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: While Cam's thunder is stolen by a popular teacher's return, Mitch doesn't know what to make of his former college pal-turned-new boss.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: A leisurely day at the museum proves to be anything but for Cam, Mitch and Manny. Plus, Phil helps Andy with his anniversary gift.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge gets breast implants meant for a younger patient, and Bart and Milhouse spot Krusty on an episode of `Batman and Robin'.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Simpsons' house has to be fumigated after an ice hockey player gives his termite-infested stick to Lisa, and the only suitable place the family can find to live is in a TV reality show about life in the 1890s.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Someone tries to murder Homer in the steam room at the Stagnant Springs Spa. Needing help from someone who understands the mind of a killer, Chief Wiggum invites Sideshow Bob out of prison to help investigate the case, but has Bob got over his compulsion to kill Bart, and can he work out who Homer could possibly have annoyed enough to provide a motive for murder?

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart feels sorry for Mrs Krabapple after Skinner stands her up again to attend to his mother's whims, so Lisa suggests he should nominate her for Teacher of the Year. However, Skinner worries that he could lose her if she wins.

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Prince is smitten with Vicky, but does she feel the same? Warren makes a chilling discovery, and Nancy reaches breaking point.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Homer gets kicked out of an all-you-can-eat seafood restaurant for eating too much, he takes the owner to court. programme.sub-title: The One with All the Greatest Moments
programme.desc: Patsy Palmer from `EastEnders' loves a good wedding and she has picked her top 20 most jaw-dropping moments from the first three series of `Don't Tell the Bride'.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Joe DiDomizio is the president and CEO of Hudson Group, a travel retailer that operates newsstands, bookshops, cafés and speciality stores in 70 airports and other transportation terminals throughout North America.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Utah Jazz CEO Greg Miller's team spirit is tested when he is asked to be a human basketball hoop while he is undercover.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon heads to Pantaleone's in Denver, Colorado, where he meets the Italian restaurant's owner Pete, who is truly stuck in the past. Pete stubbornly maintains that he makes the `Best Pizza in Denver', based on a review from the 1980s.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon heads to Arvada, Colorado, where husband-and-wife owners Randy and Alexa have allowed their Old Neighborhood restaurant to fall into utter disrepair.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying & Selling
programme.desc: A woman who has beaten cancer wants to be able to live the rest of her life to the fullest, and the boys must help her find the dream space to do just that. Jonathan turns her home into a showstopper and Drew's house hunt ends up close to square one.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In the North East, married duo Lynne and Ian, newly-weds Jess and Connor and engaged couple Tom and Sam compete in a high-energy week featuring plate smashing and a Shirley Bassey serenade.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: The contestants are forced to wrangle wild animals, with unpleasant results for Steve Pemberton and Sophie Willan. John Robins reveals a love of pickled onions, and Nick Mohammed sparks controversy with a cardboard tube.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts, while Sean Lock and Vic Reeves take on special guest captain Lee Mack with Sara Pascoe. Alex Horne and the Horne Section join Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent, while Rachel Riley takes care of the numbers and letters.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts, while Sean Lock and Mel Giedroyc take on special guest captain Lee Mack with David O'Doherty. Adam Buxton joins Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent, while Rachel Riley takes care of the numbers and letters.

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: It's the start of term and Carli is organising a charity fashion show, and like every school fashion show it's only for the coolest and best looking kids. This means our four heroes aren't involved - or at least not until the last minute when Carli's desperate, but their help proves to be a bigger hindrance than anyone could have imagined.

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: Simon is chasing a fit girl in the year below, so to impress her, he goes to his first gig since his mum took him to see Take That at Wembley Arena. Will, Jay and Neil tag along and pretty much ruin Simon's chances, but thankfully the bouncers are nice enough.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers: Extreme
programme.desc: Pash tackles Rebecca's pain in the neck, Sketch destroys Layton's dodgy design, Alice takes pity on Teresa and her kitty, and Uzzi covers Sean's crustacean situation.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: The contestants are forced to wrangle wild animals, with unpleasant results for Steve Pemberton and Sophie Willan. John Robins reveals a love of pickled onions, and Nick Mohammed sparks controversy with a cardboard tube.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts, while Sean Lock and Vic Reeves take on special guest captain Lee Mack with Sara Pascoe. Alex Horne and the Horne Section join Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent, while Rachel Riley takes care of the numbers and letters.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Anthony Wedo, president and CEO of buffet restaurant organisation Buffets, Inc., goes undercover posing as a construction worker taking part in a reality TV competition to win enough money to start up his own business.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Amit Kleinberger, CEO and co-owner of frozen yogurt chain Menchie's, goes undercover posing as a participant in a reality TV contest who is trying to win money to fund and develop a new business.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying & Selling
programme.desc: Soon-to-be empty nesters want to downsize from a sprawling five-bedroom home to a single-storey property. Their current home is bland and in desperate need of an overhaul, so Jonathan tackles an ambitious renovation to transform it into a showpiece.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In East Kent, dinner party veterans Mike and Helenor kick off the competition, followed by Rob and Jade on day two who put together a Thai-inspired menu, and the last to host are visually impaired Caroline and her easy-going husband Tony.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In South Wales, Red and Bozey, `Princess' Trish and Alan, and Marc and Dan compete in a week that features tattoos, a musical-themed feast and nana's egg sauce.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with All the Payback
programme.desc: In three years of `Don't Tell the Bride', dozens of brave brides have handed complete control of the biggest day of their lives to their blokes. In return, their grooms were given £12,000 and a chance to prove themselves to the woman they love. Did the blushing brides get the wedding they'd always dreamed of? The girls take the chance to have their say.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.sub-title: The One with the Skydiving Bride
programme.desc: John and his fiancée Jackie live in Kirkintilloch, an old mining town on the outskirts of Glasgow. Country boy John was born there and has never left, but city girl Jackie hails from Glasgow's West End and has always dreamt of a sophisticated and glamorous wedding. John has his head in the clouds for his bride's big day and wants to throw Jackie out of a plane at 10,000 feet on the morning of her wedding.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Three previously featured bosses ask employees to go under cover for a second look inside their organisations.

programme.title: Undercover Boss USA
programme.desc: Jeff Platt, president and CEO of entertainment and fitness business Sky Zone, a chain of indoor trampoline parks, goes undercover in his own company posing as a contestant on a reality TV competition who is trying to win an action-adventure holiday.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon heads to Queens, New York, where he finds a family torn apart by the stress of running failing Greek restaurant Kati Allo. Husband and wife owners Manny and Christine are on the brink of divorce, and daughter Evelyn is caught in the middle.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon heads to Woodland Park in Colorado for the first of a two-part visit to Mangia Mangia, an Italian restaurant with an identity crisis.

programme.title: Property Brothers: Buying & Selling
programme.sub-title: A Home for All in the Family
programme.desc: James and Ada are on the hunt for a spacious family home with a flat for Grandma and Grandpa, but they need to spruce up their current house of oddities before they can make the move.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Belfast, Sarah and Paddy kick things off with a fine-dining feast. The hosts mix Eastern and traditional fare for guests Louise and Danny, and Emma and Samson.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: Steve Pemberton experiments with G-force, Joanne McNally rocks a traffic cone, Sophie Willan splatters a human pigeon, Nick Mohammed gets trapped under a bed and John Robins constructs a lengthy homage to Little Alex Horne's hero.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts a new edition of the panel game with Sean Lock and a special guest captain. The teams are joined by celebrity guests. Susie Dent is in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters.

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: Simon and his new girlfriend Tara are on a double date with Will and her friend Kerry. Simon's principles are tested when he has to weigh up snogging the face off his new girlfriend, but only if he lets her redesign his wardrobe. Meanwhile, Will balances up spending time with an embarrassing and rather boring girl for the prospect of some action.

programme.title: The Inbetweeners
programme.desc: Simon and his new girlfriend Tara are going to visit her sister at university. The other boys invite themselves along - Will on the pretext that he wants to take a look around his absolute last choice of university, Jay so he can give Simon his `expert' sex advice, and Neil because he has nothing better to do.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay continues his travels across the United States, travelling to New Jersey to try to snatch Italian restaurant Leone's from the jaws of failure. Owner Leone had a thriving business, but after a sudden illness she spent two years in hospital in a coma. When she returned, she was heartbroken to discover that her lazy son Michael had run all her efforts and the restaurant into the ground.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: Harry wants a royal wedding and to be King for the day and plans to make his no-nonsense bride his Queen. But, bride-to-be Charlotte wants something low key.

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: The Hutchinson family are faced with a devastating reality. Meanwhile, Charlie prepares to face the music, and Beth extends an olive branch to her estranged brother.

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Charlie falls on his sword, but not everyone's ready to forgive and forget. Elsewhere, Misbah plays matchmaker, Frankie wants to do the right thing and Jenson supports Mercedes.

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: A short, cheeky clip from Rude Tube's naughty little sister Rude-ish Tube, featuring sheep.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Jackie and Richard's evening features some celebrity name-dropping. There's more bragging than cooking for Ross and Hanne. And Gail and Nigel take a risk: will it pay off?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Orange County, Southern California, to try to save an Italian restaurant from imminent destruction. Luigi's was a successful restaurant with a great reputation until Dominic decided to retire to Italy and brought in son Tony to help run it. Familial fighting and an unappetising menu of 126 items have let the restaurant down the drain. Can Gordon fix it, or is Luigi's beyond repair?

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: In Atlanta, Gordon helps first-time restaurant owners Richard and Jorge. Their restaurant, Park's Edge, has been open for three years but is in big trouble due to constant fighting between them, poor food and a bad vibe in the tight-knit neighbourhood. Gordon comes up with a brilliant plan to resolve the issue within the community, but then a rogue chef threatens to ruin everything.

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.sub-title: Don't Mess with My Texas Wedding

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil gets a new RV for a fun family road trip, Jay and Gloria search for Manny's backpack and Lily's gymnastics meet brings out Cam and Mitch's competitive spirit.

programme.title: Modern Family

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Krusty shuns his old routines in favour of edgier material and suddenly becomes the hottest comic in show business.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer is arrested for an insurance scam that Moe hatched to impress his new girlfriend.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge poses for a calendar for charity, but she is soon the gossip of the city after she shows a little too much during her photo shoot.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa accepts an invitation to join a coven of wiccans and soon finds herself the chief witness in their trial for witchcraft. Meanwhile, Homer starts to hang out with his new pal Cletus after discovering the slack-jawed yokel makes moonshine.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake is frustrated when a detective from Major Crimes takes over a murder case that he is close to solving and steals his thunder. Determined to outwit `The Vulture', he enlists his colleagues to help him find the murder weapon and close the case.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: Jake bets Captain Holt that he can steal his Medal of Valor before midnight and Amy must overcome her hatred of Halloween.

programme.title: Modern Family

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Luke competes against the son of Phil's nemesis in a wrestling match.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: While Cam's thunder is stolen by a popular teacher's return, Mitch doesn't know what to make of his former college pal-turned-new boss.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: A leisurely day at the museum proves to be anything but for Cam, Mitch and Manny. Plus, Phil helps Andy with his anniversary gift.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge gets breast implants meant for a younger patient, and Bart and Milhouse spot Krusty on an episode of `Batman and Robin'.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: The Simpsons' house has to be fumigated after an ice hockey player gives his termite-infested stick to Lisa, and the only suitable place the family can find to live is in a TV reality show about life in the 1890s.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Someone tries to murder Homer in the steam room at the Stagnant Springs Spa. Needing help from someone who understands the mind of a killer, Chief Wiggum invites Sideshow Bob out of prison to help investigate the case, but has Bob got over his compulsion to kill Bart, and can he work out who Homer could possibly have annoyed enough to provide a motive for murder?

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart feels sorry for Mrs Krabapple after Skinner stands her up again to attend to his mother's whims, so Lisa suggests he should nominate her for Teacher of the Year. However, Skinner worries that he could lose her if she wins.

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Prince is smitten with Vicky, but does she feel the same? Warren makes a chilling discovery, and Nancy reaches breaking point.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Homer gets kicked out of an all-you-can-eat seafood restaurant for eating too much, he takes the owner to court. The restaurant offers a peace deal: Homer can eat all the food he likes so long as people can gawk at him like a freak in return. Meanwhile, Bart falls in love with his babysitter.

programme.title: Married at First Sight USA
programme.desc: Wedding bells ring for the final pair in this year's experiment, before all five couples jet off to Panama for their honeymoons.

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Political activist Lettie knows a lot about politics but is clueless on love. Her date is Cambridge graduate Fred. There is also octogenarian Doreen, who meets author James, who's ready to write the next chapter of his love life.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Homer becomes silver screen superhero in the form of `Everyman', but he soon has trouble staying on the exercise and diet regime that his provided fitness trainer lays out for him.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Bart's mischief-making results in Mrs Krabappel receiving her P45. Can Bart clear his guilty conscience?

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: With Erica at a crossroads about her future, she draws inspiration from Baby Boom and crafts a plan to have it all; Adam remakes the "Uptown Girl" music video, meets Carmen's father and learns to be more of a "downtown man."

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Things are finally working out for Adam, that is until he receives a letter from NYU that could change everything; when Barry hears the JTP have other friends outside their crew, he fears the end of their posse as they know it.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride
programme.desc: It's high noon for impulsive groom Steve as he plans a cliff-hanger of a wild west wedding to Leona. Will Steve's nuptials on a bridge prove a swinging success?

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: It's Phil's birthday and he desperately wants the year's must-have gadget. Meanwhile, Mitchell tries to find his inner mean machine.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Phil and Jay take the reigns when Luke and Manny's hot-tempered basketball coach quits during a game. Meanwhile, Mitchell invites Cameron to meet a prospective employer.

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Sue and Shaun kick off the competition in Bournemouth with a Mediterranean menu, but one guest proves hard to please. Perfectly preened pair John and Livi add some glamour to proceedings.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Ventura Harbor in Southern California to help a family-run Greek restaurant facing big problems that hasn't taken a profit for two years. Makis Mikelatos and his wife Lynn have run the restaurant, the Greek at the Harbor, for 17 years, but Makis - who works 17 hours a day, seven days a week, and rarely takes a holiday - is exhausted, and if he doesn't slow down he's in danger of collapse.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon travels to Oakhurst, New Jersey, to help Mike Farber, the overwhelmed owner of Mike and Nellie's restaurant, who lost his way when Nellie, his father and business partner, died. Opened in 1996, the restaurant was a lifelong dream for the father and son. Alone at the helm, and still mourning the loss of his father, Mike has taken his eye off the ball and the restaurant has gone to pot.

programme.title: Modern Family

programme.title: Modern Family

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Lisa fears she is genetically doomed to lose her intelligence.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Marge invites Ralph Wiggum over for a play-date with Bart. When Bart goes back to the Wiggum household and finds it filled with police equipment and files, he is in a troublemaker's paradise.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: After meeting his millionaire half-brother, Homer sets out to design the perfect car.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Abe tells Marshall Goldman of the time he was on a World War II battleship and the story is later published in the Springfield Shopper. As a result, his Springfield popularity increases rapidly and he decides to `dump' Homer as his son.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine
programme.desc: After making an arrest without much proof, Jake has just 48 hours to collect enough evidence before the perp is released.

programme.title: Brooklyn Nine-Nine

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Jay is determined to get the adults' hotel suite upgraded. Claire tries to win back some money.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: Mitch struggles to hide his genuine feelings about an unflattering wedding topper Cam's father made. Phil tries to help Gloria sell the apartment she had before Jay but they get sidetracked at a salon. Jay wants to host a nice family evening.

programme.title: Modern Family
programme.desc: The family joins Phil as he travels to Australia to fulfil his mother's wish that he see the land where he was conceived.

programme.title: Modern Family

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.sub-title: The Dad Who Knew Too Little
programme.desc: Lisa is upset that Homer doesn't know more about her, so he hires a detective to spy on her, but when Homer fails to pay him, the detective frames Lisa for a crime and she has to go on the run.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: Inspired by Ned Flanders, Homer turns to prayer in the hope of changing his lot in life. After he is injured coming out of church, he sues for damages and is awarded the church itself.

programme.title: The Simpsons
programme.desc: When Bart discovers the existence of an embarrassing TV commercial he appeared in as a baby, and that Homer spent the money he earned, he sues for emancipation and moves out of the house.

programme.title: The Black Swan
programme.desc: With the pardon of notorious pirate Henry Morgan by the English King, Caribbean pirates are offered amnesty if they give up their lawless ways. As governor of Jamaica, Morgan picks Captain Jamie Waring to round up the renegade captain Billy Leech. Beautiful Margaret Denby complicates matters for Waring, as her scorn for him is matched only by his love for her.

programme.title: Yellow Sky
programme.desc: Seven outlaws rob a bank in Arizona then head for the badlands where they encounter an old man and his granddaughter, who have a hidden cache of gold. The outlaws make a deal to share it but some of them choose to cheat. Their leader, who has fallen for the granddaughter, takes on the double-crossers, finally facing one of them in a show-down.

programme.title: Lifeboat
programme.desc: Tensions erupt between the survivors of a sunken ocean liner and the Nazi responsible for the disaster.

programme.title: Heaven Knows, Mr Allison
programme.desc: A Roman Catholic nun and a hard-bitten US Marine are stranded together on a Japanese-occupied island in the South Pacific during World War II. Under constant threat of discovery by a ruthless enemy, they hide in a cave and forage for food together. Their forced companionship and the struggle for survival forge a powerful emotional bond between them.

programme.title: Gemini Man
programme.desc: Henry Brogan is an elite assassin who becomes the target of a mysterious operative who can seemingly predict his every move. To his horror, he soon learns that the man who's trying to kill him is a younger, faster, cloned version of himself.

programme.title: Renfield
programme.desc: Renfield, the tortured aide to his narcissistic boss, Dracula, is forced to procure his master's prey and do his every bidding. However, after centuries of servitude, he's ready to see if there's a life outside the shadow of the Prince of Darkness.

programme.title: How to Have Sex
programme.desc: Three British teenage girls go on a rites-of-passage holiday - drinking, clubbing and hooking up, in what should be the best summer of their lives.

programme.title: The Lobster
programme.desc: Single people must find romantic partners in 45 days or be transformed into beasts.

programme.title: Prue Leith's Cotswold Kitchen
programme.desc: Food campaigner Henry Dimbleby joins Prue to explain how he convinced Michelin-starred chefs to work in school kitchens as her husband John goes fishing for supper.

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Cooking
programme.desc: Nigel Slater looks at the role of contrasting temperatures in this programme, to show us how the marriage of something hot with something cold can turn a good dish into something really special. From a simple twist on the classic hot apple pie and ice cream, to a chip shop-inspired dish of sizzling fish with a lime and chilli 'vinegar', Nigel helps us to understand the role of hot and cold in cooking.

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Cooking
programme.desc: Nigel shows us that the most unlikely combinations of ingredients can often be the best, as he explores unusual ingredients and quirky twists on classic recipes. Newcomers to black pudding will be tempted by Nigel's alternative sausage roll, while lovers of the traditional bread and butter dessert will love Nigel's version, made with exotic spices and caramelised bananas.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is making non-stop all-in-one wonders! There's a simple chicken and dressing sheet pan supper, lemon, basil and shrimp risotto in a skillet, the easiest ramen pho and a layered portable Greek salad in a jar.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is fixing a warming lunch. There's chunky beef chili, a real cowboy favourite amped up with two kinds of beef, and to top it all off, the cheesiest quesadilla makes a fabulous fixin'.

programme.title: Jamie's Quick and Easy Food
programme.sub-title: Fishcakes, Linguine, Lamb Chops, Chocolate Mousse
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver makes quick Asian fishcakes, lemony courgette linguine, sticky lamb chops and a supercharged cherry chocolate mousse.

programme.title: Jamie's Quick and Easy Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver cooks up a tender lamb shoulder, super green spaghetti, one-pan fabulous fish and apple crumble cookies.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa
programme.desc: Ina assembles a gorgeous seasonal brunch. On the menu are morning glory muffins, a fresh fruit platter, shakshuka with feta, and a spicy bloody Mary.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It's Chocolate Week and the bakers tackle a deceptively simple traybake signature, a technical packed full of chocolate and nuts, and a white chocolate celebration showstopper.

programme.title: Classic Mary Berry
programme.desc: Mary returns to school to celebrate her light, easy and delicious cooking. From a simple version of the classic kedgeree and some delightful breakfast drop scones, to a moreish chicken, avocado and bacon salad and deliciously easy beef burgers. Mary takes her inspiration from a visit to a groundbreaking primary school in south London which has built a secret garden where they teach their students how to grow vegetables and look after a brood of chickens.

programme.title: James Martin's United Cakes of America
programme.desc: James Martin's great American cake adventure starts in Virginia, with a classic apple pie. He also discovers the New Orleans King Cake in a southern bakery.

programme.desc: James explores the U.S. obsession with cupcakes and samples Maryland's state dessert.

programme.title: James Martin's United Cakes of America
programme.desc: James tries a Pimlico Cake in Baltimore, before heading for the Maryland countryside to taste a cake which is only made for six weeks of the year.

programme.sub-title: Making It up Isn't Making Do
programme.desc: Food writer Nigel Slater creates a week's worth of simple yet delicious meals, using everyday ingredients to rustle up sumptuous food that is both inspiring and achievable. Nigel encourages us to experiment in the kitchen and have the confidence to make dishes up as we go along. He also visits local allotment holders and creates a tasty feast from the fruits of their labour.

programme.title: Nigel Slater's Simple Suppers
programme.desc: The remains of the Sunday roast are the starting point for Nigel, as he makes amazing food out of seemingly uninspiring leftovers. It may sound frugal, but Nigel uses his culinary creativity to show why he is one of the nation's favourite foodies. He also visits local allotment holders, and is challenged to cook something on the plot using the freshest of ingredients.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is making a spicy food delivery and spilling the hot sauce on the food her family loves. There's her daughter's favourite, Kung Pao chicken, and spicy Sriracha popcorn is a winner with the entire family.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: It's time for evening meals in Ree's 16-minute frontier fast lane! On the menu are speedy shrimp tacos, pork chops with wine and garlic, zesty lemony green beans, unbeatable pepperoni chicken and crispy grilled cheese sandwiches.

programme.desc: Jamie Oliver makes high-speed ginger shakin' beef, crispy squid and smashed avocado, pork and mash gratin, and almond, orange and polenta cake.

programme.title: Classic Mary Berry
programme.desc: Mary embraces the British countryside with fresh and honest bucolic cooking inspired by what is grown on farms and in gardens, from a joyous pasta inspired by a classic French herb sauce, to a perfect way to partner asparagus, and how to serve up the most scrumptious roasted vegetables. programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James' British adventure has crossed over the Irish sea to Northern Ireland, where he is joined by his great pal and famous local chef, Paul Rankin. Their first port of call is the Giant's Causeway, where James settles right in and cooks up a fish stew for the crowds of tourists. Paul goes in search of Irish gold and finds it in the form of rapeseed at the Kane family farm in Limavady.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James and Paul are having a blast in Northern Ireland and they've reached Paul's home town of Belfast, where he's giving James a personal tour of Northern Ireland's capital city before they head off for a spot of golf. James pitches up his mobile kitchen right on the green to whip up a tasty dish with bacon, scallops and apples with onion rings. After all his exertion on the golf course, Paul heads just out of town to Carnbrooke Meats.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin has a hair-raising experience at a barber's in Rochdale, Lancashire, while Martin Roberts looks round a great bungalow in Alsager, Cheshire, and Lucy Alexander sees all the potential in a piece of land in Eccles in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a house in Otterton, Devon, which is given a stunning transformation to match the views from the windows, Lucy Alexander looks round a small one-bedroom flat in Tottenham, London, and Dion Dublin peers into a building in Alvaston, Derby, which is in a dangerous state of disrepair.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a house in Otterton, Devon, which is given a stunning transformation to match the views from the windows, Lucy Alexander looks round a small one-bedroom flat in Tottenham, London, and Dion Dublin peers into a building in Alvaston, Derby, which is in a dangerous state of disrepair.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin visit a one-bedroom flat in Catford, London, a large house near the River Mersey and a semi-detached property in Alvaston in Derby.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An end-terrace house in Alfreton in Derby bought by two brothers, a mid-terrace on the coast at Seaham in County Durham and a large three-storey property with a flat and shop in Taylorstown in the Rhondda Valley are all sold at auction. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin talk to the new owners to find out what they have planned for their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts falls in love with a quirky cottage in Malvern bought by a builder and his wife, Dion Dublin talks to a first-time property developer who sees her end-terrace house in Chell in Stoke-on-Trent as something for her pension pot, and Lucy Alexander meets a divorced couple who have decided to form a new union as property developers doing up a shop and flat in Dover, Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A large piece of land in Ratby in Leicestershire, a terrace in Chesterton in Staffordshire and a 1930s semi with a huge garden and huge potential are all sold at auction. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the developers hoping to breathe new life into their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A two-bedroom detached house in Basford in Nottingham, a top floor flat in Portsmouth and a large Edwardian terrace in Crewe are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and hear about their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A multimillion-pound development in Farningham in Kent, an ambitious, total renovation project in Aspatria in Cumbria and a very small studio flat in Wallington in Surrey are all sold under the hammer. Martin and Lucy meet the new owners to discuss their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A Victorian terrace in London's Greenwich, two flats in Chatham in Kent and a riverside inn near Great Yarmouth in Norfolk are all sold under the hammer. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the new owners and hear plans for their renovation projects.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A three-bedroom house in Borrowash in Derbyshire, another three-bedder in West Bromwich in the Midlands and a quirky four-bedroom house on the Isle of Sheppey are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat in Pegswood in Northumberland, a terrace in Swadlincote in Derbyshire and a former shop in Chatham in Kent are given makeovers.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A mid-terrace house in Wales, a basement office in central London with a huge price tag and two flats in Bootle near Liverpool are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy meets a young couple expecting a baby who have to weigh up whether to live in their auction buy in Sydenham in London while Martin talks to a man and his father in law who have bought their first property together in Trentham near Stoke and Dion meets a man who only went into property development after he retired. His latest buy is a three-bed semi in West Bromwich.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Terraced houses in Tunstall in Shropshire and Prescott in Liverpool and a large flat in Dagenham are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion have a look round before hearing what the new owners have planned.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A chiropodist, two part-time gardeners and a woman who works on projects in between the school run are today's investors. They've bought property in Bullbridge in Derbyshire, Newcastle under Lyme in Staffordshire and Wolverhampton in the West Midlands under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts follows the progress of a couple developing a chalet bungalow in Drakelowe in Staffordshire for their family, while Lucy Alexander talks to a young entrepreneur with grand plans for a three-bed semi in Croydon in London. Dion Dublin looks round a piece of land which runs alongside a tourist steam train track in Coltishall in Norfolk.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts rolls back the clock nearly 12 years to catch up on an ambitious development of a former church in Preston on the Hill in Cheshire, while Lucy Alexander looks round two very different terraced houses in Stoney Stratford, near Milton Keynes, and Streatham in London.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A 1930s terrace in Croydon, a semi in Wolstanton destined to be a family home and the only house on a street in Moorside in Oldham are all under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion visit the properties and meet the new owners.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin visits a stone cottage in Tilton on the Hill in rural Leicestershire while Martin Roberts is at a property auction buy in Croydon on the outskirts of London and Lucy Alexander meets two friends who have bought a bungalow in Rainham in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A mid-terrace house in Chaddesdon in Derby, a two-bedroom flat in London's Maida Vale and a property in Warrington are all sold under the hammer. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the new owners to hear what they have planned for their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin looks round a pretty cottage with outbuildings with huge potential in Burton upon Trent, while Lucy checks out a flat in London's Crystal Palace and Dion is in Mossley near Manchester in a four-bedroom bungalow.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin looks round a pretty cottage with outbuildings with huge potential in Burton upon Trent, while Lucy checks out a flat in London's Crystal Palace and Dion is in Mossley near Manchester in a four-bedroom bungalow.

programme.desc: The relatives of murderers tell their stories, revealing how the crimes affected their families and whether there were any signs of murderous intentions beforehand.

programme.desc: When a stolen vehicle hits and kills a veteran officer, it takes a massive agency manhunt to bring the criminals down. In South Carolina, an officer learns that three bank robbers are coming his way, and they do not intend to surrender quietly.

programme.title: High Speed Chase
programme.desc: In Florida, a boat thief runs from the law, kicking off an adrenaline-fueled speedboat chase. A night-time patrol shifts up a gear when a six-ton U-Hual suspected of harbouring stolen goods is spotted.

programme.desc: Andrew Pearson kidnaps his ex-partner Natalie Harker before strangling, drowning, and sexually assaulting her. In a distraught 999 call, he then tries to claim her death was an accident, leaving a series of clues as he speaks.

programme.title: Crime Scene Confidential
programme.desc: Alina Burroughs is eager to determine whether Michelle Witherell accidentally fell from her balcony, committed suicide, or was murdered; searching for the truth, she navigates a maze of conflicting pathology reports.

programme.title: Signs of a Psychopath
programme.desc: A lone gunman shoots a young man and woman on their way home from the gym where they work. While interrogating a suspect, detectives learn that this is no random attack but the twisted plan of a deranged psychopathic stalker.

programme.title: Signs of a Psychopath
programme.desc: A woman's body is found strangled near a country road. Investigators learn the suspect is a prolific serial killer who raped and murdered eight women. They look to track down the murderer before he kills again.

programme.title: Signs of a Psychopath
programme.sub-title: I Guess My Life Is Over Now

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street

One day she finds a strange, well dressed woman at her doorstep, Susan Williamson, who claims to be an old sweetheart of Jean's husband Derek, but he says he has never seen her before. Meanwhile, the Australian couple Len and Julie Wilcox is in Aidensfield to look for relatives.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The residents of Milton Cross, a feudal estate with a church, farms, houses and acres of land, depend on lord of the manor Edward Milton for their livelihoods. When Sonia Woodley is stabbed in the churchyard two years after the death of her abusive husband Gerald, Barnaby and Jones investigate - and the village starts to reveal its secrets.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emmerdale is one of Britain's best-loved and highly-viewed long-running drama soaps - set in the heart of the beautiful countryside of the Yorkshire Dales, this realistic and down-to-earth drama superbly chronicles the joys and the heartbreaks of a tightly-knit countryside community.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The story of everyday life in a small and tightly-knit community - this is the core of the world's longest running prime-time drama serial and the highest-rating programme in the UK. Coronation Street has for over 50 years uniquely combined cult status with popularity and durability - the quality of writing is second to none, portraying believable characters with a large helping of humour.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The story of everyday life in a small and tightly-knit community - this is the core of the world's longest running prime-time drama serial and the highest-rating programme in the UK. Coronation Street has for over 50 years uniquely combined cult status with popularity and durability - the quality of writing is second to none, portraying believable characters with a large helping of humour.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Greengrass is visited by his brother, whom he hasn't spoken to in many years, while Nick tries to keep the peace among the villagers.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick has suspicions when a lay preacher is injured in a shooting accident and more trouble looks set to arise when Greengrass comes across a secret recipe for the famous Aidensfield bun.

programme.title: Grace
programme.desc: As Grace's team recovers from recent distressing events, they are called to the scene of an awful car accident when local hotelier Bryson Lambert is killed by an oncoming driver who cruelly flees the site of the crash and leaves him to die.

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: A murder at Esme's office shocks the Keating family; Alphy's meeting with the bishop doesn't go as planned.

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Alphy and Geordie's investigation into the murder of an archaeologist uncovers a web of betrayal and deceit.

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: When a rape and murder case goes to trial, DCI Gill Murray is determined to see the defendant, Georgios Stelikos, convicted, but the case is made more complicated because only one of the alleged victims is prepared to testify. When Rachel gives evidence, she is horrified to discover that her ex Nick is the defence barrister. Nick's cross examination shakes her when he uses personal information.

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: When porn actress Vicky Birkinshaw is arrested on suspicion of murdering her husband, Rachel and Janet are convinced that she is hiding the truth, but even they are shocked by the revelations to come. Rachel's professional life hangs in the balance when DCI Gill Murray finds out about her relationship with barrister Nick Savage. Janet's affair with a colleague is also uncovered, and Rachel confronts Janet about it.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien seek out old item to fix up and turn for profit. Henry restores two quad bikes and a sun lounger while Simon rescues a canoe and turns an oak beam into a spice rack.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien visit Essex, where Henry restores a car trailer and a 1970s racing bike, and Simon turns his attentions to lovingly restoring a vintage model aircraft.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: This week's competition starts in Barmouth, north Wales, at the Bae Abermaw Hotel, run by newly engaged couple Rosie Lewis and Dexter Green. On arrival, guest Tracy finds herself in a hairy situation.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Rigsby's Guest House in Doncaster, South Yorkshire, run by Tracy Ward. The lack of en-suite bathrooms causes a stink, and some guests cannot figure out the pricing.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit of the week is to The Somerset Arms in Semington, Wiltshire, where John Adams is joined by friend Dennis Gowie. But cleanliness issues and thin walls gave cause for concern.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to The Cross Keys Hotel, Saffron Walden, Essex run by former flight attendant Nici Van Valen. The guests love the luxury but a mark on Tracy's towel leaves a stain on her stay and tensions are at boiling point.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It is the final day of the competition and the owners are meeting for the last time to find out what they have been paid and to settle some scores. After tension around the table, the payments are revealed and the winner is finally announced.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: From Perth the first to host is 38-year old first aid manager Jacqui. Jacqui plans to impress her guests with hearty cuisine and is serving up a selection of pates, roast venison and a homely sticky toffee pud.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the second day of the competition in and around Perth and the turn of aspiring actor Daniel to host. Daniel is hoping to impress with his twist on a traditional Scottish menu, serving Spaghetti Hagginaise for his main and home-made Irn Bru ice cream for dessert.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The third instalment of the week's brand competition comes from Basque-born Lorea and she's on a mission to showcase the culinary delights of her native home in Northern Spain. Lorea's fiesta commences with a pink cocktail and an exotic quartet flown over from the Basque country especially to impress.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the fourth night of the competition in and around Perth and the turn of feisty Jenna to try and impress. She is aiming for a night of perfection, but last night's host Lorea is baffled by her decision to put plastic on her dining room floor.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the final day of this competition in and around Perth and it's the turn of Kiwi-born Craig to host. Craig is hoping that his land and sea menu can blow the competition out of the water. He's gone for locally caught prime lobster and has had wild boar brought in especially from the Scottish highlands.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition starts in Harrogate where management team Chris `Clarky' Clarke and Katie Light help run the White Hart Hotel. The guests meet some llamas and the extra charge for fancy coffee causes a stir.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to New Promenade Hotel in Blackpool, owned by Raouf, who is joined by friend Chrissie. There's a teetering toilet seat and Clarky and Katie find muck on the taps.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The third visit is to Vicky's small terraced house, aptly named The Terrace, in Oxford. On inspection, two pairs of guests discover an array of filthy finds, including a pre-sucked sweet.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit is to Robin and Liza's Georgian mansion Tall John's House in the Brecon Beacons. At dinner the guests gush over their new friendships but Vicky doesn't share their feelings.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: It's the final day of the competition and the B&B owners are meeting for the last time to settle some scores. The White Hart Hotel is up first and management team Clarky and Katie want to park the issue of paying for their car park.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer catch up with mum and daughter Linda and Laura, who were looking for a Bristol bolthole, and Ranald and Marjorie, who were upsizing in Glasgow.

programme.title: PopMaster TV
programme.desc: Ken Bruce hosts the third heat, as more music enthusiasts are put to the test. Who can come out top of the charts and secure their place on the leaderboard?

programme.title: Kevin McCloud's Listed Britain
programme.desc: From the gothic fantasy of Castell Coch to Liverpool's Philharmonic Dining Rooms' marble loos, why is Britain so good at saving the buildings of the powerful?

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Mark is brought in after coming off his bike and doctors are concerned that he may have suffered life-changing damage to his spine. Dibs, who is 60, has fallen 10 feet off a balcony onto concrete and comes in with his wife Di.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Cyclist Ollie, who's 16, has been in a traffic accident. Dominic's throat has been bleeding badly after he had his tonsils out. Narayan's six. He has paper stuck deep in his ear.

programme.title: Snakes in the City
programme.desc: Scorch and Slither: The hotter the day, the more deadly the snakes. That's why Simon and Siouxsie find their lives on the line when they get called out to catch a black mamba.

programme.title: When Sharks Attack
programme.desc: Usually a pristine location, the waters of Cancun suddenly become a hot spot for shark attacks between 2011 and 2013, with six attacks along the shores; scientists work to figure out the cause behind the surge in attacks.

programme.title: Wild Fish
programme.desc: Cyril and his team embark on a dangerous mission to find the white sturgeon.

programme.title: The Incredible Dr. Pol
programme.desc: To heal their patients the Vets need to get dirty. Nastiness awaits. Dr. Pol and his team tackle infected kittens, a stinky Macaw, and an oozy calf.

programme.title: Yukon Vet
programme.desc: Dr. Oakley gets a puppy count on a pregnant dog.

programme.title: Animal Fight Club
programme.desc: Life in the wild is a constant battle and animals go to incredible lengths to survive. When they face off, there are no prizes for second place.

programme.title: Red Richardson: At the Rich Mix
programme.desc: Standup comedian Red Richardson performs at Rich Mix in London on 16 October 2020.

programme.title: David Crowe: White Bread
programme.desc: David Crowe is a 20 year veteran of the comedy stage. He won both the Seattle and San Francisco Comedy Competitions and has performed for a US President.

programme.title: Mike P. Burton: I Can Get Whiter
programme.desc: Mike Burton delivers his comedy with high energy, great storytelling, and hilarious facial expressions.

programme.title: Johnny Beehner: The Flamingo Special
programme.desc: Johnny Beehner has everything people want from a comic: He's silly, he's likeable, and he's fun.

programme.title: Moody McCarthy: The Good Jew
programme.desc: Comedian Moody McCarthy performs in front of a live audience.

programme.title: Mike Toomey: Welcome to My World
programme.desc: Mike Toomey has been a favorite at comedy clubs and special events since 1982.

programme.title: Lamuweri Guluka: The First Option
programme.desc: As a nerdy, suburban, black male, Lamuweri has a unique comedic perspective that audiences love.

programme.title: Lee Hardin: Not Quite A Grown Up
programme.desc: Lee Hardin is known for making bad decisions and his love for Taco Bell.

programme.title: Mike Guido: Sanity Optional
programme.desc: Comedian Mike Guido performs a standup show in front of a live audience.

programme.title: Kelly Terranova: A Man Named Kelly
programme.desc: Kelly Terranova entertains with expressive observational humor.

programme.title: Mike Siegel: Have Jokes, Will Travel
programme.desc: Stand-up comedian, TV host and actor Mike Siegel performs different acts and entertains with hilarious takes on numerous subjects.

programme.title: Keith Barany: Dude... Seriously?
programme.desc: There's no doubt Keith Barany delivers the laughs, every single time he hits the stage. As a tested and veteran touring pro, Keith could explain for hours about the subtle skill set he employs to make the laughs happen.

programme.title: Mike Siegel: Have Jokes, Will Travel
programme.desc: Stand-up comedian, TV host and actor Mike Siegel performs different acts and entertains with hilarious takes on numerous subjects.

programme.title: Dan Megini: Been There, DUMB that!
programme.desc: Comedian Dan Megini performs a standup set.

programme.title: Chris Strait: Laugh Till You Run Out of Air
programme.desc: No funny topic is off-limits for this California native, but he's just telling his story.

programme.title: Karen Mills: Pink Pants
programme.desc: Karen Mills has made a career out of making people laugh; she's been a nationally touring comedian for 25 years.

programme.title: Joey ILO: Just Work With What You Got
programme.desc: Joey ILO takes to the stage to cracks joke with his iconic relatable style.

programme.title: Jimmy Della Valle: Famous Nobody
programme.desc: Jimmy delivers hilarious stories and one-liners from his unique, streetwise point of view.

programme.title: Fred Klett: One of Ten
programme.desc: Fred Klett showing off his clean comedy trademark, appealing to families with members of all ages with material taken from his experiences growing up in a large Christian family.

programme.title: Rex Havens: Women and Children First
programme.desc: Rex has been delighting comedy audiences for 20 years, in world-famous comedy clubs like The Improv, Catch-A-Rising Star, Funny Bones and Zanies, on television for Showtime, Comedy Central and Comic Relief, on radio with Bob & Tom and NPR, cruise ships worldwide, and corporate clients including GM, American Express, Texaco, 3M, Coca-Cola, IBM, Toshiba.

programme.title: Warren B. Hall: Mostly Kid Stuff
programme.desc: Simply put, Warren B Hall is not what the viewer is expecting. He purposely avoids using the white vs black angle done by many mainstream black comedians.

programme.title: Max & Ivan: Holmes & Watson
programme.desc: The world's greatest detective tackles Chicago's murky underworld. A prohibition-era tale of revenge, whisky and javelins.

programme.title: Juliette Burton: Defined
programme.desc: Juliette Burton goes on her journey of self discovery (and mental health recovery) in asking: why can't we be everything at once?

programme.title: Andrea Hubert: Week
programme.desc: Hubert's debut show is a candid, upfront hour of observational comedy with her struggles with mental health in the foreground from the off.

programme.title: Jake Donaldson: Neurotica
programme.desc: Jake Donaldson is a male comedian who just wants to be a good feminist, but he grew up in the 90s and has a `proper man' for a dad, so now he feels bad about not being able to put up a shelf.

programme.title: Njambi McGrath: Breaking Black
programme.desc: Njambi McGrath offers acerbic anecdotes about life in Kenya, living in a white neighbourhood, why immigrants are attracted to the UK, and how they thrive amongst certain hostilities.

programme.title: Becky Brunning: Bloom
programme.desc: Bright, breezy and a breath of fresh air, Becky's feel good story-telling on love, sexuality, feminism, and embarrassing dads is all charm.

programme.title: Holly Burn: The Best of The Burn
programme.desc: Daredevil absurdist Holly Burn creates a whole host of obscure characters and semi-reckless stunts.

programme.title: Jenna Kim Jones: Fun to Hug
programme.desc: Jenna Kim Jones is a comedian known for her strong personality and little to no upper body strength. She recently came to grips with the fact that she will never be able to do a pull up and earn her 4th grade Presidential Fitness Award though she plans to exploit this limitation into a book or reality television deal.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: Peppa is on a play date with Candy Cat at Mr Cat's Cafe. Candy show Mr Cat the drawings they've done of everyone, and he hangs them up for everyone to see.

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: On a cold day, Daddy Pig suggests they build a den until Mummy Pig has finished work. After a hard day's work, the cushion den is a perfect place to watch TV together!
