The residents are terrified of the gunslinger but live in greater fear of a gang which intends to wipe out the whole town.

programme.title: Blue Moon
programme.desc: In 1943 lyricist Lorenz Hart confronts his shattered self-confidence in a bar as his former collaborator, Richard Rodgers, celebrates the opening night of his groundbreaking hit musical "Oklahoma!"

programme.title: The Cinema List: Bingeable
programme.desc: On the Cinema List, join us as we explore our favourite franchises to get cosy with, from Transformers to Bad Boys 2. Snuggle down on the sofa and take your pick.

programme.title: Mean Machine
programme.desc: A footballer is jailed for throwing a crucial game. Whilst inside, the football-loving warden sets him the task of coaching a team of prisoners to take on the all-conquering warders' side. Can he form a disparate bunch of prisoners into a winning team?

programme.title: Am I OK?
programme.desc: When Lucy realises that she's never been attracted to men, her lifelong friend Jane helps her navigate coming out in her 30s.

programme.title: The First Wives Club
programme.desc: When Brenda Cushman, Elise Elliot and Annie Paradis are reunited at the funeral of a college friend, they discover they have another thing in common: they've all been dumped by their husbands, Morty, Bill and Aaron, for a younger model. Vowing revenge, they decide to hit them where it hurts most: the pocket. Using help and their various skills, they begin to pick them off one by on.

programme.title: Weird Science
programme.desc: Two high-school nerds accidentally create the woman of their dreams when an electrical storm zaps their home computer.

programme.title: Blue Moon
programme.desc: In 1943 lyricist Lorenz Hart confronts his shattered self-confidence in a bar as his former collaborator, Richard Rodgers, celebrates the opening night of his groundbreaking hit musical "Oklahoma!"

programme.title: Instant Family
programme.desc: When Pete and Ellie decide to start a family, they stumble into the world of foster care adoption. They hope to take in one small child, but when they meet three siblings, including a rebellious 15-year-old girl, they find themselves speeding from zero to three kids overnight. Now, Pete and Ellie must try to learn the ropes of instant parenthood in the hope of becoming a family.

programme.title: Mean Girls
programme.desc: After 15 years in African bush country, Cady knows all about herd instincts, but nothing has prepared her for high school. She joins a popular but cruelly manipulative crowd of girls known as the `Plastics' and secretly mocks them, but the fun stops when one betrays her and steals the boy she fancies.

programme.title: Anchorman 2: The Legend Continues
programme.desc: Ron Burgundy tries to stay classy when he and his team take New York and the nation's first 24-hour global cable news network by storm.

programme.title: You've Got Mail
programme.desc: Two rival bookstore owners are unaware that they are conducting a secret romance with each other on the Internet. When they discover the truth, they must prevent their business rivalry from interfering with their growing personal intimacy.

programme.title: Miss Congeniality
programme.desc: An uncouth FBI agent goes undercover in the Miss United States beauty pageant as a contestant to flush out a terrorist who plans to bomb the event. The hard-nosed cop, who prides herself on her toughness, must discard her trainers and feminise her behaviour if she is to avoid blowing her cover.

programme.title: Miss Congeniality 2: Armed and Fabulous
programme.desc: FBI agent Gracie is given another makeover to become the face of the Bureau, prior to embarking on a promotional tour. When two of her friends are kidnapped - Miss USA and a beauty pageant director - she decides to investigate on her own, in defiance of orders to the contrary.

programme.title: Weird Science
programme.desc: Two high-school nerds accidentally create the woman of their dreams when an electrical storm zaps their home computer.

programme.title: Coyotes
programme.desc: Members of a family find themselves in a desperate fight for survival when a pack of coyotes swarms their Hollywood Hills home.

programme.title: Coyotes
programme.desc: Members of a family find themselves in a desperate fight for survival when a pack of coyotes swarms their Hollywood Hills home.

programme.title: On Swift Horses
programme.desc: Muriel and her husband, Lee, begin a new life together after he returns home from the Korean War. However, their newfound stability gets upended by the arrival of Lee's charismatic brother, a wayward gambler with a secret past.

programme.title: Girl You Know It's True
programme.desc: Grammy Award-winning music act Milli Vanilli become the first act to have their prize stripped from them after they are exposed as a fake enterprise with the singers lip-syncing to pre-recorded tracks.

programme.title: Beach Rats
programme.desc: An aimless teen in Brooklyn, NY, spends the summer with his delinquent buddies, a potential new girlfriend and older men that he meets online.

programme.title: Plainclothes
programme.desc: In the 1990s, an undercover officer is told to entrap and apprehend gay men only to find himself drawn to one of his targets.

programme.title: Babylon
programme.desc: The story of Blue, a young man of Jamaican descent living in Brixton in 1980, as he hangs out with his friends, fronts a dub sound system, loses his job, struggles with family problems and has his friendships tested by racism.

programme.title: Black Dog
programme.desc: Released from jail, Lang returns to his hometown in Northwest China. As part of a dog patrol tasked with clearing stray dogs before the 2008 Olympics, he bonds with a black stray. The two lonely souls embark on a new journey together.

programme.title: It Ends With Us
programme.desc: Lily Bloom falls for a charming neurosurgeon after she moves to Boston to chase her dream of opening her own business. However, when her first love suddenly reenters her life, her relationship gets upended, leaving her with an impossible choice.

programme.title: Full Metal Jacket
programme.desc: A US Marine observes the effects the Vietnam War has on his fellow Marines from their brutal basic training to the bloody street fighting in Hue, Vietnam.

programme.title: A Big Bold Beautiful Journey
programme.desc: Through a surprising twist of fate, single strangers Sarah and David get to relive important moments from their respective pasts, illuminating how they got to where they are in the present.

programme.title: Legends of the Fall
programme.desc: Sweeping epic about three sons raised on a Montana ranch by their father, a colonel who has turned his back on the army after the Indian Wars. Though the destinies of the boys are very different, two forces impact on each of their lives: the Great War in Europe and the love of a young woman. Covering several decades, the action ranges from majestic Montana to the battlefields of Europe and adventure on the high seas.

programme.title: A Different Man
programme.desc: An aspiring actor undergoes a radical medical procedure to drastically transform his appearance. However, his new dream face quickly turns into a nightmare as he becomes obsessed with reclaiming what was lost.

programme.title: Aisha
programme.desc: Caught in limbo for years in Ireland's immigration system, a young Nigerian woman develops a friendship with an employee who she meets at one of the accommodation centres.

programme.title: Black Dog
programme.desc: Released from jail, Lang returns to his hometown in Northwest China. As part of a dog patrol tasked with clearing stray dogs before the 2008 Olympics, he bonds with a black stray. The two lonely souls embark on a new journey together.

programme.title: Mouse Hunt
programme.desc: Slapstick tale which follows the misfortunes of two hard-up brothers whose plans to renovate and sell a valuable building are jeopardised by a rogue mouse. The brothers soon find themselves pulling out all the stops in order to evict their tiny resident.

programme.title: Rebellious
programme.desc: A headstrong princess is stolen by an evil sorcerer, and her scholarly fiancé must use his wits in an epic quest to find her while she boldly challenges her captors and plots a daring escape.

programme.title: Bookworm
programme.desc: Eleven-year-old Mildred's world is turned upside down when her estranged father, a washed-up magician, comes back to look after her and takes her camping to find the mythological Canterbury Panther.

programme.title: The Last Airbender
programme.desc: Following the adventures of Yang who is a successor in to a long line of Avatars. He has to put aside his childhood in order to stop he Fire Nation from enslaving the Water, Earth and Air nations.

programme.title: grow
programme.desc: An exuberant tale full of giant pumpkins, madcap characters and a little girl who just might be a pumpkin-growing savant. (2025, 107 mins)

programme.title: Lemony Snicket's A Series of Unfortunate Events
programme.desc: Three orphans are sent to live with their uncle Count Olaf, but he only wants their family fortune. What follows is a battle of wits.

programme.title: Despicable Me 4
programme.desc: Gru welcomes a new member to the family, Gru Jr., who's intent on tormenting his dad. However, their peaceful existence soon comes crashing down when criminal mastermind Maxime Le Mal escapes from prison and vows revenge against Gru.

programme.title: The Wild Robot
programme.desc: Shipwrecked on a deserted island, a robot named Roz must learn to adapt to its new surroundings. Building relationships with the native animals, Roz soon develops a parental bond with an orphaned gosling.

programme.title: Jumanji
programme.desc: Alan Parrish disappeared as a young boy. Nobody believes his friend's story that he was sucked into the game they were playing. Twenty-six years later, two children find the game in their attic, and when they start to play, Alan is released, but the game isn't over yet, and Alan must finish before he is really free.

programme.title: The Bad Guys 2
programme.desc: Reformed criminals Mr. Wolf, Mr. Snake, Mr. Piranha, Mr. Shark and Ms. Tarantula find themselves hijacked into a high-stakes heist that's masterminded by a new team of delinquents they never saw coming -- the Bad Girls.

programme.title: The Boss Baby
programme.desc: A wildly imaginative seven-year-old boy discovers that his new brother, Boss Baby, is actually a spy on a secret mission, and only he can help thwart a dastardly plot that involves an epic battle between puppies and babies.

programme.title: Shrek the Third
programme.desc: Shrek is just starting to settle down in his beloved swamp with his wife Fiona, but their mucky bliss doesn't last for long as word arrives that the ogre's froggy father-in-law is about to croak. The very last thing Shrek wants is to become King, but with no other heir in sight, it looks like the grumpy green one is destined never to live quietly in the swamp again. A glimmer of hope appears in the form of heir Artie.

programme.title: Rebellious

programme.title: A League of Their Own

programme.title: Escape to Victory

programme.desc: Meet the unlikely collaborators working to ensure mutual survival in the Kalahari Basin. Learn of an elephant clan's symbiotic link to fruiting anaboom trees, a meerkat's alliance with a feisty drongo bird, and more flora-and-fauna odd couples.

programme.title: Africa's Wild Horizons
programme.desc: The Eastern Cape Province is investing heavily in ecotourism and conservation as an alternative to fishing and commercial development. The region is reclaiming farmlands and closing fishing grounds for wildlife. This is one of the most exciting conservation success stories in Africa.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: A look at the lives of monkeys, chimps, orangutans, and apes living at the world's biggest sanctuary, the 65-acre Monkey World Ape Rescue Centre in Wareham, Dorset.

programme.title: Monkey Life

programme.title: America's Wild Seasons
programme.desc: In autumn, change sweeps across America. As the trees greet the chill with a blaze of color, it's make or break time for animals. Time to pull up stakes, make last minute preparations, and often find a mate before the cold sets in.

programme.title: Kalahari: Land of Secret Alliances
programme.desc: Thirst for rain alongside the animals of the Kalahari as elephant herds cooperate and meerkat's adopt a strange babysitter in the depths of a brutally dry year.

programme.title: New Kids in the Wild
programme.desc: Join baby capuchin monkey Chico in Brazil as he explores a forest full of excitement and danger and learns key skills that will allow him to survive and thrive.

programme.title: Patrick Aryee's Wild World
programme.desc: Join Patrick in the Kruger National Park, where he is exploring whether ivory poaching can lead to food shortages for local people.

programme.title: Rooted
programme.desc: The Namaqua Rock Fig Tree stands rooted on the slope of a rocky hill in Namaqualand, South Africa. It is the ultimate survivor, able to withstand the unforgiving conditions in a landscape facing ever rising temperatures and less and less rainfall each year.

programme.desc: A typhus epidemic strikes in New Providence and results in the kidnapping of a Spanish doctor when a quarantine is imposed.

programme.desc: Released from prison after serving eight years, Bill Weldon sets out to kill the three men who testified against him.

programme.title: Cash on Demand
programme.desc: A crook abducts the wife and child of a bank manager & then schemes to rob the institution.

programme.title: Film Noir

programme.desc: The classic game show Give us a Clue hosted by Michael Parkinson. Celebrity guests Jay Aston, Bobby Gee and Roy Kinnear mime iconic titles and phrases to compete and win points!

programme.desc: A young girl's body found near a busy motorway tells of something sinister.

programme.desc: Ex-police detective Lomax is released from prison and returns to London to find that his wife has emigrated, and his son is missing.

programme.title: Murder by Decree
programme.desc: Sherlock Holmes finds clues implicating some members of the British Parliament in the Jack the Ripper murders.

programme.title: Angel on My Shoulder
programme.desc: A murdered gangster makes a deal with the devil, agreeing to take over and corrupt the identity of a crusading judge, in return for having his murder avenged.

programme.title: Sing As You Swing
programme.desc: Top British radio stars perform in this musical revue.

programme.title: Port of Escape
programme.desc: A chance encounter between two men aboard a ship leads to mystery and murder.

programme.title: Tom Brown's Schooldays
programme.desc: New boy Tom enters Victorian Rugby and faces headmaster Dr. Arnold and the bully Flashman.

programme.title: Four Star Playhouse
programme.desc: A man whose wife is ill begins a love affair with his secretary, but he soon receives news that his wife is coming home.

programme.desc: A group of Fenians are plotting activity in the area. Sergeant Cork gets a warning from an unreliable source.

programme.title: The Adventures of Sir Lancelot

programme.title: Cinebox Memories
programme.desc: Italian saxophonist Riccardo Rauchi and his ensemble of musicians perform Okay Madison.

programme.title: The End of the Road

programme.title: Doctor in Distress
programme.desc: When a fiery, overbearing consultant surgeon suddenly mellows, the hospital staff do not know whether to be pleased or alarmed. Has he fallen in love? The young doctor adds his chief's love life to the catalogue of medical duties that make up his day.

programme.title: Railway Murders
programme.desc: Investigating the most notorious murders ever to take place on the British railways. Nicholas Day narrates the history of blood on the tracks of Britain.

programme.title: The Brothers
programme.desc: Fearing bankruptcy, Ted is sent to locate Tom Parker and Barbara's trip to Paris is not all she wanted it to be.

programme.title: The Wheeltappers and Shunters Social Club

programme.title: Confessions From a Holiday Camp
programme.desc: The lifestyle of a holiday camp worker is jeopardised when the place is taken over by a former prison warder with his own ideas of how things should be run.

programme.title: Ship of Fools
programme.desc: Passengers on an ocean liner headed from the Americas to Germany reflect a world headed for Nazi influence.

programme.title: Doomwatch

programme.desc: Quilley and Skinner arrest drug pusher Jill Conrad. During a search of her flat a quantity of heroin is found and she is arrested. Former detective Neil Goss, now a solicitor, represents her and claims it was they who planted the drugs.

programme.title: Burke's Law
programme.desc: Amos searches for the person who substituted belladonna for the Grand Duke's medication.

programme.title: Great Raids of World War II
programme.desc: It was Hitler's deadliest secret. His scientists had promised him an atomic bomb. If he got it first, Nazi domination was assured. In 1943, a team of Norwegian raiders was sent to destroy a depot in the heart of Norway where heavy water, the one thing needed to complete the bomb, was produced. This documentary explores the raid, with veterans' accounts, archive film, reconstructions, and 3-D graphics.

programme.title: How the War was Won: The Victory Years

programme.desc: The war in the Pacific nears its violent end as a drastic tactic triggers the nuclear age. The brave men share their thoughts behind the war's most tragic events.

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and the team present the makeover show, transforming unloved spaces into dream gardens for some very special people. In this episode, they visit Moorland in Somerset - an area which was devastated by the floods of 2014. There they surprise a couple whose lives - and garden - were turned upside down by the disaster. Sheila Colquhoun and her husband Alex were looking forward to retirement that year, but instead it turned out to be one of the worst in their lives.

programme.title: Rick Stein's India
programme.desc: Rick arrives in the royal state of Rajasthan, the land of the kings. The majority of Rajasthanis are serious meat eaters with dishes like Junglee Maas, a perfect combination of meat, chillies and spices, easily accessible on game hunts - the favourite pastime of both the Rajputs and Moghuls. This is a land of palaces and hilltop forts, mostly desert and very different from the state of Himachal Pradesh.

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for previously unseen material, and a stellar cast of celebrity guests reveal amazing stories about themselves. But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up? Among the guests joining Lee Mack and David Mitchell are James Acaster, David Baddiel, Ed Balls, Mark Bonnar, Jo Brand, Steve Davis, Susie Dent, Ade Edmondson, Sheila Hancock, Claude Littner, Cariad Lloyd, Gabby Logan, Stephen Mangan, Bob Mortimer, Melvin Odoom, and Ore Oduba.

programme.title: The Americas with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels from Texas and down the length of Mexico. In Texas, Simon joins the US Border Patrol, who constantly pick up migrants who have illegally crossed the dangerous Rio Grande river in search of a new life in the United States. In the midst of this humanitarian crisis, Simon meets one woman who risks her life to take vital supplies across the border to stranded migrants.

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Dave and Si celebrate Britain's taste for the exotic, one of the driving forces of our culinary history. They prepare a classic chilli ginger bread and show how to make the perfect sushi, travel to Tatton Park in Cheshire to see how the Pineapple was once the most expensive and exotic dish available to polite society, and reveal the history behind a piece of exoticism closer to home: the Onion Johnnie Man.

programme.title: Rick Stein's India
programme.desc: Rick arrives in the royal state of Rajasthan, the land of the kings. The majority of Rajasthanis are serious meat eaters with dishes like Junglee Maas, a perfect combination of meat, chillies and spices, easily accessible on game hunts - the favourite pastime of both the Rajputs and Moghuls. This is a land of palaces and hilltop forts, mostly desert and very different from the state of Himachal Pradesh.

programme.desc: Fred Dibnah continues his tour of Britain's great building feats with a look at our places of work. In Chatham he visits one of our oldest dockyards, while at Armley Mills in Leeds he discovers an early form of fireproofing.

programme.title: Fred Dibnah's Magnificent Monuments

programme.title: River Walks
programme.desc: Father and daughter Stanley and Rachel Johnson, who both grew up in the Exe valley, walk the first 12 miles of this beautiful river. They start their journey from the headwaters high on Exmoor and finish in the picture-postcard village of Winsford.

programme.title: World at War
programme.desc: After Hitler's initial territorial gains in Russia, the relentless advance of the Wehrmacht is decisively stopped at Stalingrad. For the first time in the war, the Nazis suffer heavy losses in what will later be regarded as one of the decisive turning points of the conflict.

programme.title: World at War
programme.desc: The German attempts to starve Britain by attacking ships bringing supplies from North America are very nearly successful. Many tonnes of shipping are sunk and many lives are lost to U-Boat attacks. Defeat in the Atlantic would surely mean the end of the war for the Allies.

programme.title: The World at War
programme.desc: A documentary series that gives a historical account of the events of World War II, from its roots in the 1920s to the aftermath and the lives it profoundly influenced.

programme.title: World at War
programme.desc: While Bomber Command are unable to hit their targets precisely and begin bombing German cities under cover of darkness, the Americans reinforce the daytime attacks.

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Madge and Mavis are about to leave the area. Granville sets up a big night out in a pricey restaurant so he and Gastric can persuade their lady loves to stay. It's just a pity he's now got to introduce her to his father.

programme.desc: The wedding of Nurse Gladys Emmanuel's niece is cause for consternation and embarrassment when Arkwright loses his trousers.

programme.title: Black Ops
programme.desc: Dom and Kay are two ordinary police community support officers on the front line of policing, handing out frisbees with 'Stay Street Smart' printed on them in east London. programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Finding themselves unexpectedly in a country ditch, Foggy, Clegg and Compo decide that one standard gent's bicycle doesn't really meet their combined transportation needs. programme.desc: Gladys falls for Clive, but Maplin announces her engagement to Spike.

programme.desc: The platoon take advantage of Mainwaring's absence as they take part in a darts match in the pub against the ARP Wardens. When Captain Mainwaring returns unexpectedly, Wilson has to try and get them out.

programme.title: Are You Being Served?
programme.desc: When the standard of canteen food goes from bad to worse, the staff decide to run the canteen themselves.

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Madge and Mavis are about to leave the area. Granville sets up a big night out in a pricey restaurant so he and Gastric can persuade their lady loves to stay. The only problem is that Granville's money belt seems to be tightening every time anyone looks at the menu. Meanwhile, Leroy's romance with Beth finally seems to be getting somewhere. It's just a pity he's now got to introduce her to his father.

programme.title: Open All Hours
programme.desc: The wedding of Nurse Gladys Emmanuel's niece is cause for consternation and embarrassment when Arkwright loses his trousers.

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: The platoon take advantage of Mainwaring's absence as they take part in a darts match in the pub against the ARP Wardens. When Captain Mainwaring returns unexpectedly, Wilson has to try and get them out.

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: A chance encounter with an unwilling and resentful dog-walker suggests to Foggy that this might be the business opportunity he has been waiting for all his life. With the reluctant assistance of Compo and Clegg, he sets out to prove that `walkies' could mean big money for them.

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Finding themselves unexpectedly in a country ditch, Foggy, Clegg and Compo decide that one standard gent's bicycle doesn't really meet their combined transportation needs. They set about constructing two further bicycles from spare parts and rigorously road testing them for design faults.

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: When Hyacinth and Richard go to stay in a country cottage for a couple of days, events take several unexpected turns.

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Captain Mainwaring sets up an observation post in the lighthouse, thus providing ample opportunity for disaster, especially with Jones in charge and an impending air raid.

programme.title: Open All Hours: Inside Out
programme.desc: Diane Morgan narrates this special documentary about the much-loved comedy, featuring contributions from Sir David Jason, Johnny Vegas, Tim Healy, Ronnie Barker's daughter Charlotte, and more.

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville takes delivery of a bargain consignment from Spain. Eric and Cyril fall out over a lost compass and Mr Newbold falls in and gets soaked on a boat trip with Mrs Rossi.

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: After Lucy suffers a mild indiscretion whilst drunk and claims to have no memory of the event, she and Lee embark on an experiment with a bottle of home-made potato hooch to prove once and for all that you can remember what happens when you are mashed.

programme.title: Not Going Out
programme.desc: Lee's past catches up with him when a young woman appears on his doorstep and asks for a cup of sugar.

programme.title: Still Open All Hours
programme.desc: Granville takes delivery of a bargain consignment from Spain. Eric and Cyril fall out over a lost compass and Mr Newbold falls in and gets soaked on a boat trip with Mrs Rossi.

programme.title: Hi-De-Hi!
programme.desc: Gladys falls for Clive, but Maplin announces her engagement to Spike.

programme.title: Don't Wait Up
programme.desc: Tom decides he cannot have his father staying with him any longer, but Toby also has other things on his mind.

programme.title: Black Ops
programme.desc: Dom and Kay are two ordinary police community support officers on the front line of policing, handing out frisbees with 'Stay Street Smart' printed on them in east London. Detective Inspector Clinton Blair needs two undercover officers to infiltrate the Brightmarsh Gang, but the Met chiefs don't seem to get the brief, so Clinton decides to take matters into his own hands and recruits Dom and Kay in an 'off-the-books' operation.

programme.title: The Outlaws
programme.desc: The outlaws try to launder their dirty money before the gangsters track them down.

programme.title: Men Behaving Badly
programme.desc: Dermot is trying to impress Deborah by offering her his body. Gary is trying to impress her by installing a burglar alarm. Which is the better offer?

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Presented by Dara Ó Briain, `Mock the Week' is a comedy programme that combines the best elements of panel show, stand-up and improvised games with two teams of comedians taking a satirical swipe at the news and world events.

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O'Briain and Hugh Dennis are joined by James Acaster, Angela Barnes, Ed Gamble, Darren Harriott and Zoe Lyons.

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: British sitcom about a married couple struggling with surreal events in suburbia.

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: June helps Terry to practise to be the auctioneer at a local charity event.

programme.title: Terry and June
programme.desc: After an attempted break-in, Terry makes his own burglar alarm.

programme.title: Terry and June

programme.title: Terry and June

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Piers Morgan travels to Lowell Correctional Institution in Florida to meet Rebecca Fenton, found guilty for the brutal murder of her husband.

programme.title: Deadly Waters With Captain Lee
programme.desc: In the wake of a violent storm, a Rhode Island harbormaster finds a badly decomposed body aboard a sailboat. Now, detectives must uncover the truth: Was the killer Mother Nature or a more nefarious force?

programme.title: The Gentle Art of Swedish Death Cleaning
programme.sub-title: The Death Cleaners vs. The Sister Council
programme.desc: Flora and Jeff, finally empty-nesters after raising eight kids, are ready to death clean, but their kids are against it because they want their childhood home just like they remember it.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: V and Ollie blue light to a rugby pitch to help a man who had a seizure. Plus, Stef and Lee treat an excitable 6-year-old Spider-Man fighting an asthma attack.

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Ceramicist Izzy from London and stone carver Luke from the Scottish Borders step into the marquee hoping to turn their passions and skills into successful businesses. It's high stakes for Izzy as she's already given up her job in advertising to try and make it as a full time potter. For Luke, dyslexia, and years of being bullied at school have knocked his confidence, preventing him from putting himself and his pieces out there, but Dom and mentor Louis can see just how talented he is.

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Dom welcomes willow weaver Dea and artist Gemma into the marquee. With chronic fatigue syndrome along with autism and ADHD, Dea dreams of being able to build a home business that suits her life. After being told art doesn't pay by a careers advisor at school, Gemma gave up on her artistic dreams and became a teacher. She continued to create art as a hobby and after some recent success selling her art at a market she's hoping mentor Sharon can help her finally become a full-time artist.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A widow reluctantly parts with her husband's treasured collection of Rolls Royces and Bentleys, and an impulse buyer finds one of the cars a new home.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A near-perfect 50s Chevy panel van and a very desirable low-mileage 80s Brooklands Capri tempt auctioneer Derek out of Yorkshire.

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: By 1943, life was good for many of the SS. Eyewitness accounts recall how those in power at Auschwitz lined their pockets with wealth stolen from Jewish inmates while also engaging in illicit affairs.

programme.title: Rise of the Nazis
programme.desc: Adolf Hitler had been chancellor of Germany for just under a year and it was a challenging balancing act. On the one hand the Nazis had to be be mindful of president Paul von Hindenburg and the vice chancellor Franz von Papen, who were members of Germany's traditional aristocratic and ruling elite.Then, there was the Nazis' own power base, the stormtroopers, made up of millions of angry, disenfranchised men who wreaked havoc on the streets of Germany.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce takes the team to the Lancashire coast for a busy day in the magnificent Blackpool Tower Ballroom. Objects under discussion include a bible containing handwritten extracts from Charlotte Bronte; powerful images drawn under fire by a war artist; and a boot sale brooch that makes the owner want to scream with delight when she hears the valuation.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return for a second visit to the Tower Ballroom in Blackpool. Amongst the objects under the experts' eye are one of the earliest prayer books seen on the programme and a valuable pair of duelling pistols, while the original Teddy from Watch with Mother makes a surprise appearance.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek heads to Scotland in the mother of all rainstorms to pick up a Jaguar E-type and a rare MGC convertible. Plus, Dave extracts a rare Austin Gipsey from a lock up.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: There's a surprise waiting for Derek in Hull - a MGSA sports car that once had a tree growing through it. Plus, in North Wales, Alan Holmes is selling his son's Ford Escort RS 1600i.

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: By 1943, life was good for many of the SS. Eyewitness accounts recall how those in power at Auschwitz lined their pockets with wealth stolen from Jewish inmates while also engaging in illicit affairs.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: A fallen tree and a leaking lock pound hamper Robbie's journey across the Pennines.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie runs aground on the Rochdale Canal, will he make it to Manchester?

programme.title: Train Truckers
programme.desc: A CBD80 battery-powered loco needs to be hauled 20 miles from a factory in Burton-on-Trent to the heritage Chasewater Railway in Staffordshire.

programme.title: Trawlermen: Hunting the Catch
programme.desc: 300,000 sq miles of ocean. Three GPS-tracked voyages, filmed around the clock. One week at sea. Emilia Jayne hunts for cuttlefish in the world's busiest shipping lane. Cargo ships have right of way here, but lucrative cuttlefish swim in these grounds too. After finding good fishing at the start of his week-long trip, can skipper Luke Selvey keep the secret to himself? Margaret of Ladram is Brixham's most successful trawler.

programme.desc: Derek heads to Scotland in the mother of all rainstorms to pick up a Jaguar E-type and a rare MGC convertible. Plus, Dave extracts a rare Austin Gipsey from a lock up.

programme.title: Flipping Bangers
programme.desc: Gus and Will find an Audi Quattro roadster that has seen better days. It is full of problems and has not seen a service in years. Clearly, they need to buy it.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien find, fix and flog unwanted possessions. At the fairground, the boys pick up a bicycle made for two, a ship's search lamp and a lambing chair.

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James goes off-road to tell the story of how the 4x4 conquered the world. From the wartime jeep and the global struggle between Land Rover and Land Cruiser, to the white-knuckle world of rallying and boy racers. James conquers Mount Fuji and races through the sun scorched Mojave Desert to decide which is the ultimate people's 4x4.

programme.desc: At the peak of his power, Hitler now faces fellow ruthless dictator Stalin. He tries to deceive Stalin with a plan to invade Britain, all while preparing an attack on the Soviet Union.

programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return with a second helping from a recent visit to Hertford College, Oxford. Objects under the cameras include a bracelet once worn by a princess with a tragic story; expensive ceramics bought for a song at a boot sale; and a rare document recording the end of hostilities in the First World War. Plus, one of the experts shows his hand as an accomplished amateur potter.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team head for the beautiful Tamar Valley in Devon, and weigh anchor at Morwellham Quay. Objects fascinating the experts include an ugly brown jug with a handsome valuation, a stunning and rare silver salver, and a long case clock arrives in unexpected fashion.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek picks up a rare Ford Zodiac Farnham Estate, while Dave heads for an isolated tent in the Midlands to collect a Triumph Toledo.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: Auschwitz: The Nazis and the Final Solution
programme.desc: This episode tells the history of the most frenzied period of killing in the history of Auschwitz and the surprising story of the Nazis' attempt to negotiate with the Allies.

programme.title: James May's Cars of the People
programme.desc: James explores the wilder shores of motoring to discover what happened to the cars of the future that we were promised. From improbable steam cars and ludicrous jet turbines he reveals how the petrol engine and the power it gave us came to dominate the 20th century. He'll take to the waves in an amphibious car, risk his life at the wheel of a notorious electric scooter and take a hair raising trip in the fastest driverless car on earth.

When Laxman Singh conditionally permits the British to use the port for trading purposes, Sangha finds out that the British are taking out gold and jewellery and bringing in ammunition, so he sets out to put a stop to this.

programme.title: Saaransh
programme.desc: BB Pradhan, a retired headmaster, has to confront the tragedy of his only son's death in New York, alongside his grieving wife, Parvati. Without their son's income, the couple decide to rent a room but soon find themselves involved in the troubled affairs of their young guest, Sujata, with dangerous and far-reaching consequences.

programme.title: Henna
programme.desc: Chander is a laidback Kashmiri Hindu in love with Chandni. On his way to see his fiancée, Chander's car meets with an accident and falls into a river. He washes up on the other side of the border with no memory of who he is. Assuming he is a Pakistani Muslim like them, the villagers who find him name him Chand and take him into their fold. While there, he falls in love with a local village girl, Henna.
